[[Category:History of Fukui Prefecture]] [[Category:Sabaemabe family]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Manabe clan]] [[Category:Clans in the Kanto region]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq deprecated open]] [[Category:Manabe clan]][[de:Manabe (Klan)]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[sn:Manabe clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Manabe clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Manabe clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Manabe clan]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:間部氏]] {{Infobox Japanese clan | name = Manabe Clan | crest = Marunimitsuhikiryo.svg | crest caption = [[Mitsuhiki-ryō within a circle|Maru ni Mitsuhiki]] | home province = [[Echizen Province|Echizen]] ([[Sabae Domain|Nishi Sabae]]) | main branch = [[Manabe clan (main branch)|Main]] | founder = [[Manabe Akimitsu]] | dissolved = | type = [[Samurai]]
[[Kazoku]] (''[[Shishaku]]'') | final ruler = [[Manabe Akinobu]] | noteworthy members = [[Manabe Akifusa]], [[Manabe Akikatsu]] | branches = Honjo Manabe clan ([[Samurai]])
Akasaka Manabe clan ([[Samurai]]) }} The '''Manabe clan''' (間部氏, ''Manabe-shi'') was a [[Samurai]] and later [[Kazoku]] clan of [[Japan]]. Its rise to prominence began in the mid-[[Edo period]] with the elevation of [[Manabe Akifusa]] to [[daimyō]] status. Following the [[Meiji Restoration]], the family became part of the [[Kazoku]] peerage system as ''shishaku'' (''viscount''). The clan's hereditary title included the character 詮 (''sen''). == History == Originally, the clan bore the name "Shiokawa" {{Sfn|Kansei Genealogies|1923|p=708}}. Under Okabe Akimitsu, they adopted "Manabe" after his mother's family, later altering the name to "Manabe" {{Sfn|Kansei Genealogies|1923|p=708}}. During the generation of Kiyosada, a descendant of Akimitsu, the family temporarily adopted the surnames Hoshino and Nishida before reverting to Manabe {{Sfn|Kansei Genealogies|1923|p=708}}. Manabe Akimitsu's father, Manabe Sadaaki, is believed to have been part of a branch of the Manabe family, which ruled the local stronghold in [[Izumi Province]], at Tannowa. Kiyosada's eldest son, Akifusa, served under Tokugawa Tsunayoshi (later [[Tokugawa Ienobu|Ienobu]]), during which time the surname officially changed to Manabe. As Ienobu became shōgun, Akifusa rose as a powerful aide, but his influence declined following the death of the 7th shōgun, [[Tokugawa Ietsugu]]. With the ascension of [[Tokugawa Yoshimune]], Akifusa was reassigned to the [[Murakami Domain]] in [[Echigo Province]]. His successor, [[Manabe Akitoki]], was later transferred to the [[Sabae Domain]] in Echizen Province, marking the clan's final territorial base. Key figures in the clan's history include Akifusa, a prominent shogunal advisor, and [[Manabe Akikatsu]], who served as a senior [[Rōjū|Elder of the Bakufu]]. The last daimyō, [[Manabe Akimichi]], oversaw the clan's transition during the [[Meiji period]]. After the [[Abolition of the han system|abolition of the domains]] in 1871, the Manabe family was incorporated into the Kazoku system. In 1884, under the [[Kazoku|Kazoku peerage system]], the family received the rank of ''shishaku'' (viscount) {{Sfn|Kodate|2006|p=336}}. Under [[Manabe Akinobu]], the family resided in [[Hamadera]], [[Osaka Prefecture]] {{Sfn|Peerage Register|1990|p=194}}. == Lineage of Heads == # [[Manabe Akifusa]] (''従四位下''). Lord of [[Echizen Province|Echizen]]. # [[Manabe Akitoki]] (''従五位下''). Lord of [[Shimōsa Province|Shimōsa]]. # [[Manabe Akimichi]] (''従五位下''). Lord of [[Tango Province|Tango]]. # [[Manabe Akinaka]] (''従五位下''). Lord of [[Shūzen]]. # [[Manabe Akishige]] (''従五位下''). Lord of Shimōsa. # [[Manabe Akihiro]] (''従五位下''). Lord of [[Wakasa Province|Wakasa]]. # [[Manabe Akinobu]] (''従五位下''). Lord of Shūzen. # [[Manabe Akikatsu]] (''従四位下''). Lord of Shimōsa. # [[Manabe Akitomo]] (''従五位下''). Lord of [[Awa Province|Awa]]. # [[Manabe Akimichi]] (''正五位''). Last Daimyō; first viscount. # [[Manabe Akinobu]] (''正三位''). Viscount {{Sfn|Peerage Register|1990|p=194}}. == Genealogy == ;Bold indicates the head of the clan, solid lines denote direct descendants, and dotted lines denote adoption. :Note: Some sources attribute the Manabe lineage to the [[Fujiwara clan]], specifically the Northern Fujiwara's Yamakage line. {{familytree/start}} {{familytree |01|藤原山蔭|01|[[Fujiwara no Yamakage|Yamakage]]}} {{familytree | |!||||||}} {{familytree |01|中正|01|[[Fujiwara no Nakazumi|Nakazumi]]}} {{familytree | |!||||||}} {{familytree |01|塩川満任|01|[[Shiokawa Mitsutō|Mitsutō]]}} {{familytree | |!||||||}} {{familytree |01|満任|02|[[Manabe Sadaaki|Sadaaki]]}} {{familytree | |!||||||}} {{familytree |01|詮光|01|[[Manabe Akimitsu|Akimitsu]]}} {{familytree | |!||||||}} {{familytree |01|詮房|02|[[Manabe Akifusa|Akifusa]]}} {{familytree | |:||||||}} {{familytree |01|詮勝|01|[[Manabe Akikatsu|Akikatsu]]}} {{familytree/end}} == References == * Kansei Genealogies (1923), Vol. 8. Digital archive by the National Diet Library. * Kodate, Yūji. ''Kazoku: The Aristocracy of Modern Japan''. Chūō Kōronsha, 2006. * Peerage Register, 1990 Edition. Nihon Toshokan Center. * Sabae City Historical Archives, ''Manabe Family Documents'', Vol. 1, 1980. == External Links == * {{Kotobank|Manabe clan|2=改訂新版　[[世界大百科事典]]}} [[Category:ppp]] [[Category:suQqqqqq]][[sn:Manabe clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Manabe clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Manabe clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Manabe clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Manabe clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Manabe clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Manabe clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]][[sn:anabe clan]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Removing this excess]] [[Category:qqqq]]

[[Category:History of Fukui Prefecture]] [[Category:Sabaemabe family]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Manabe clan]] [[Category:Clans in the Kanto region]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Manabe clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Manabe clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Manabe clan]][[de:Manabe (Klan)]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[sn:Manabe clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Manabe clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Manabe clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Manabe clan]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:間部氏]] {{Infobox Japanese clan | name = Manabe Clan | crest = Marunimitsuhikiryo.svg | crest caption = [[Mitsuhiki-ryō within a circle|Maru ni Mitsuhiki]] | home province = [[Echizen Province|Echizen]] ([[Sabae Domain|Nishi Sabae]]) | main branch = [[Manabe clan (main branch)|Main]] | founder = [[Manabe Akimitsu]] | dissolved = | type = [[Samurai]]
[[Kazoku]] (''[[Shishaku]]'') | final ruler = [[Manabe Akinobu]] | noteworthy members = [[Manabe Akifusa]], [[Manabe Akikatsu]] | branches = Honjo Manabe clan ([[Samurai]])
Akasaka Manabe clan ([[Samurai]]) }} The '''Manabe clan''' (間部氏, ''Manabe-shi'') was a [[Samurai]] and later [[Kazoku]] clan of [[Japan]]. Its rise to prominence began in the mid-[[Edo period]] with the elevation of [[Manabe Akifusa]] to [[daimyō]] status. Following the [[Meiji Restoration]], the family became part of the [[Kazoku]] peerage system as ''shishaku'' (''viscount''). The clan's hereditary title included the character 詮 (''sen''). == History == Originally, the clan bore the name "Shiokawa" {{Sfn|Kansei Genealogies|1923|p=708}}. Under Okabe Akimitsu, they adopted "Manabe" after his mother's family, later altering the name to "Manabe" {{Sfn|Kansei Genealogies|1923|p=708}}. During the generation of Kiyosada, a descendant of Akimitsu, the family temporarily adopted the surnames Hoshino and Nishida before reverting to Manabe {{Sfn|Kansei Genealogies|1923|p=708}}. Manabe Akimitsu's father, Manabe Sadaaki, is believed to have been part of a branch of the Manabe family, which ruled the local stronghold in [[Izumi Province]], at Tannowa. Kiyosada's eldest son, Akifusa, served under Tokugawa Tsunayoshi (later [[Tokugawa Ienobu|Ienobu]]), during which time the surname officially changed to Manabe. As Ienobu became shōgun, Akifusa rose as a powerful aide, but his influence declined following the death of the 7th shōgun, [[Tokugawa Ietsugu]]. With the ascension of [[Tokugawa Yoshimune]], Akifusa was reassigned to the [[Murakami Domain]] in [[Echigo Province]]. His successor, [[Manabe Akitoki]], was later transferred to the [[Sabae Domain]] in Echizen Province, marking the clan's final territorial base. Key figures in the clan's history include Akifusa, a prominent shogunal advisor, and [[Manabe Akikatsu]], who served as a senior [[Rōjū|Elder of the Bakufu]]. The last daimyō, [[Manabe Akimichi]], oversaw the clan's transition during the [[Meiji period]]. After the [[Abolition of the han system|abolition of the domains]] in 1871, the Manabe family was incorporated into the Kazoku system. In 1884, under the [[Kazoku|Kazoku peerage system]], the family received the rank of ''shishaku'' (viscount) {{Sfn|Kodate|2006|p=336}}. Under [[Manabe Akinobu]], the family resided in [[Hamadera]], [[Osaka Prefecture]] {{Sfn|Peerage Register|1990|p=194}}. == Lineage of Heads == # [[Manabe Akifusa]] (''従四位下''). Lord of [[Echizen Province|Echizen]]. # [[Manabe Akitoki]] (''従五位下''). Lord of [[Shimōsa Province|Shimōsa]]. # [[Manabe Akimichi]] (''従五位下''). Lord of [[Tango Province|Tango]]. # [[Manabe Akinaka]] (''従五位下''). Lord of [[Shūzen]]. # [[Manabe Akishige]] (''従五位下''). Lord of Shimōsa. # [[Manabe Akihiro]] (''従五位下''). Lord of [[Wakasa Province|Wakasa]]. # [[Manabe Akinobu]] (''従五位下''). Lord of Shūzen. # [[Manabe Akikatsu]] (''従四位下''). Lord of Shimōsa. # [[Manabe Akitomo]] (''従五位下''). Lord of [[Awa Province|Awa]]. # [[Manabe Akimichi]] (''正五位''). Last Daimyō; first viscount. # [[Manabe Akinobu]] (''正三位''). Viscount {{Sfn|Peerage Register|1990|p=194}}. == Genealogy == ;Bold indicates the head of the clan, solid lines denote direct descendants, and dotted lines denote adoption. :Note: Some sources attribute the Manabe lineage to the [[Fujiwara clan]], specifically the Northern Fujiwara's Yamakage line. {{familytree/start}} {{familytree |01|藤原山蔭|01|[[Fujiwara no Yamakage|Yamakage]]}} {{familytree | |!||||||}} {{familytree |01|中正|01|[[Fujiwara no Nakazumi|Nakazumi]]}} {{familytree | |!||||||}} {{familytree |01|塩川満任|01|[[Shiokawa Mitsutō|Mitsutō]]}} {{familytree | |!||||||}} {{familytree |01|満任|02|[[Manabe Sadaaki|Sadaaki]]}} {{familytree | |!||||||}} {{familytree |01|詮光|01|[[Manabe Akimitsu|Akimitsu]]}} {{familytree | |!||||||}} {{familytree |01|詮房|02|[[Manabe Akifusa|Akifusa]]}} {{familytree | |:||||||}} {{familytree |01|詮勝|01|[[Manabe Akikatsu|Akikatsu]]}} {{familytree/end}} == References == * Kansei Genealogies (1923), Vol. 8. Digital archive by the National Diet Library. * Kodate, Yūji. ''Kazoku: The Aristocracy of Modern Japan''. Chūō Kōronsha, 2006. * Peerage Register, 1990 Edition. Nihon Toshokan Center. * Sabae City Historical Archives, ''Manabe Family Documents'', Vol. 1, 1980. == External Links == * {{Kotobank|Manabe clan|2=改訂新版　[[世界大百科事典]]}} [[Category:ppp]] [[Category:suQqqqqq]][[sn:Manabe clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Manabe clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Manabe clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Manabe clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Manabe clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Manabe clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Manabe clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]][[sn:anabe clan]][[Category:11]] [[Category:qqqq]]

Under [[Manabe Akinobu]], the family resided in [[Hamadera]], [[Osaka Prefecture]] {{Sfn|Peerage Register|1990|p=194}}. == Lineage of Heads == # [[Manabe Akifusa]] (''従四位下''). Lord of [[Echizen Province|Echizen]]. # [[Manabe Akitoki]] (''従五位下''). Lord of [[Shimōsa Province|Shimōsa]]. # [[Manabe Akimichi]] (''従五位下''). Lord of [[Tango Province|Tango]]. # [[Manabe Akinaka]] (''従五位下''). Lord of [[Shūzen]]. # [[Manabe Akishige]] (''従五位下''). Lord of Shimōsa. # [[Manabe Akihiro]] (''従五位下''). Lord of [[Wakasa Province|Wakasa]]. # [[Manabe Akinobu]] (''従五位下''). Lord of Shūzen. # [[Manabe Akikatsu]] (''従四位下''). Lord of Shimōsa. # [[Manabe Akitomo]] (''従五位下''). Lord of [[Awa Province|Awa]]. # [[Manabe Akimichi]] (''正五位''). Last Daimyō; first viscount. # [[Manabe Akinobu]] (''正三位''). Viscount {{Sfn|Peerage Register|1990|p=194}}. == Genealogy == ;Bold indicates the head of the clan, solid lines denote direct descendants, and dotted lines denote adoption. :Note: Some sources attribute the Manabe lineage to the [[Fujiwara clan]], specifically the Northern Fujiwara's Yamakage line. {{familytree/start}} {{familytree |01|藤原山蔭|01|[[Fujiwara no Yamakage|Yamakage]]}} {{familytree | |!||||||}} {{familytree |01|中正|01|[[Fujiwara no Nakazumi|Nakazumi]]}} {{familytree | |!||||||}} {{familytree |01|塩川満任|01|[[Shiokawa Mitsutō|Mitsutō]]}} {{familytree | |!||||||}} {{familytree |01|満任|02|[[Manabe Sadaaki|Sadaaki]]}} {{familytree | |!||||||}} {{familytree |01|詮光|01|[[Manabe Akimitsu|Akimitsu]]}} {{familytree | |!||||||}} {{familytree |01|詮房|02|[[Manabe Akifusa|Akifusa]]}} {{familytree | |:||||||}} {{familytree |01|詮勝|01|[[Manabe Akikatsu|Akikatsu]]}} {{familytree/end}} == References == * Kansei Genealogies (1923), Vol. 8. [[Category:History of Fukui Prefecture]] [[Category:Sabaemabe family]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Manabe clan]] [[Category:Clans in the Kanto region]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Manabe clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Manabe clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Manabe clan]][[de:Manabe (Klan)]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[sn:Manabe clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Manabe clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Manabe clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Manabe clan]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:間部氏]] {{Infobox Japanese clan | name = Manabe Clan | crest = Marunimitsuhikiryo.svg | crest caption = [[Mitsuhiki-ryō within a circle|Maru ni Mitsuhiki]] | home province = [[Echizen Province|Echizen]] ([[Sabae Domain|Nishi Sabae]]) | main branch = [[Manabe clan (main branch)|Main]] | founder = [[Manabe Akimitsu]] | dissolved = | type = [[Samurai]]
[[Kazoku]] (''[[Shishaku]]'') | final ruler = [[Manabe Akinobu]] | noteworthy members = [[Manabe Akifusa]], [[Manabe Akikatsu]] | branches = Honjo Manabe clan ([[Samurai]])
Akasaka Manabe clan ([[Samurai]]) }} The '''Manabe clan''' (間部氏, ''Manabe-shi'') was a [[Samurai]] and later [[Kazoku]] clan of [[Japan]]. Its rise to prominence began in the mid-[[Edo period]] with the elevation of [[Manabe Akifusa]] to [[daimyō]] status. Following the [[Meiji Restoration]], the family became part of the [[Kazoku]] peerage system as ''shishaku'' (''viscount''). The clan's hereditary title included the character 詮 (''sen''). == History == Originally, the clan bore the name "Shiokawa" {{Sfn|Kansei Genealogies|1923|p=708}}. Under Okabe Akimitsu, they adopted "Manabe" after his mother's family, later altering the name to "Manabe" {{Sfn|Kansei Genealogies|1923|p=708}}. During the generation of Kiyosada, a descendant of Akimitsu, the family temporarily adopted the surnames Hoshino and Nishida before reverting to Manabe {{Sfn|Kansei Genealogies|1923|p=708}}. Manabe Akimitsu's father, Manabe Sadaaki, is believed to have been part of a branch of the Manabe family, which ruled the local stronghold in [[Izumi Province]], at Tannowa. Kiyosada's eldest son, Akifusa, served under Tokugawa Tsunayoshi (later [[Tokugawa Ienobu|Ienobu]]), during which time the surname officially changed to Manabe. As Ienobu became shōgun, Akifusa rose as a powerful aide, but his influence declined following the death of the 7th shōgun, [[Tokugawa Ietsugu]]. With the ascension of [[Tokugawa Yoshimune]], Akifusa was reassigned to the [[Murakami Domain]] in [[Echigo Province]]. His successor, [[Manabe Akitoki]], was later transferred to the [[Sabae Domain]] in Echizen Province, marking the clan's final territorial base. Key figures in the clan's history include Akifusa, a prominent shogunal advisor, and [[Manabe Akikatsu]], who served as a senior [[Rōjū|Elder of the Bakufu]]. The last daimyō, [[Manabe Akimichi]], oversaw the clan's transition during the [[Meiji period]]. After the [[Abolition of the han system|abolition of the domains]] in 1871, the Manabe family was incorporated into the Kazoku system. In 1884, under the [[Kazoku|Kazoku peerage system]], the family received the rank of ''shishaku'' (viscount) {{Sfn|Kodate|2006|p=336}}. Under [[Manabe Akinobu]], the family resided in [[Hamadera]], [[Osaka Prefecture]] {{Sfn|Peerage Register|1990|p=194}}. == Lineage of Heads == # [[Manabe Akifusa]] (''従四位下''). Lord of [[Echizen Province|Echizen]]. # [[Manabe Akitoki]] (''従五位下''). Lord of [[Shimōsa Province|Shimōsa]]. # [[Manabe Akimichi]] (''従五位下''). Lord of [[Tango Province|Tango]]. # [[Manabe Akinaka]] (''従五位下''). Lord of [[Shūzen]]. # [[Manabe Akishige]] (''従五位下''). Lord of Shimōsa. # [[Manabe Akihiro]] (''従五位下''). Lord of [[Wakasa Province|Wakasa]]. # [[Manabe Akinobu]] (''従五位下''). Lord of Shūzen. # [[Manabe Akikatsu]] (''従四位下''). Lord of Shimōsa. # [[Manabe Akitomo]] (''従五位下''). Lord of [[Awa Province|Awa]]. # [[Manabe Akimichi]] (''正五位''). Last Daimyō; first viscount. # [[Manabe Akinobu]] (''正三位''). Viscount {{Sfn|Peerage Register|1990|p=194}}. == Genealogy == ;Bold indicates the head of the clan, solid lines denote direct descendants, and dotted lines denote adoption. :Note: Some sources attribute the Manabe lineage to the [[Fujiwara clan]], specifically the Northern Fujiwara's Yamakage line. {{familytree/start}} {{familytree |01|藤原山蔭|01|[[Fujiwara no Yamakage|Yamakage]]}} {{familytree | |!||||||}} {{familytree |01|中正|01|[[Fujiwara no Nakazumi|Nakazumi]]}} {{familytree | |!||||||}} {{familytree |01|塩川満任|01|[[Shiokawa Mitsutō|Mitsutō]]}} {{familytree | |!||||||}} {{familytree |01|満任|02|[[Manabe Sadaaki|Sadaaki]]}} {{familytree | |!||||||}} {{familytree |01|詮光|01|[[Manabe Akimitsu|Akimitsu]]}} {{familytree | |!||||||}} {{familytree |01|詮房|02|[[Manabe Akifusa|Akifusa]]}} {{familytree | |:||||||}} {{familytree |01|詮勝|01|[[Manabe Akikatsu|Akikatsu]]}} {{familytree/end}} == References == * Kansei Genealogies (1923), Vol. 8. After the [[Abolition of the han system|abolition of the domains]] in 1871, the Manabe family was incorporated into the Kazoku system. In 1884, under the [[Kazoku|Kazoku peerage system]], the family received the rank of ''shishaku'' (viscount) {{Sfn|Kodate|2006|p=336}}. Under [[Manabe Akinobu]], the family resided in [[Hamadera]], [[Osaka Prefecture]] {{Sfn|Peerage Register|1990|p=194}}. == Lineage of Heads == # [[Manabe Akifusa]] (''従四位下''). Lord of [[Echizen Province|Echizen]]. # [[Manabe Akitoki]] (''従五位下''). Lord of [[Shimōsa Province|Shimōsa]]. # [[Manabe Akimichi]] (''従五位下''). Lord of [[Tango Province|Tango]]. # [[Manabe Akinaka]] (''従五位下''). Lord of [[Shūzen]]. # [[Manabe Akishige]] (''従五位下''). Lord of Shimōsa. # [[Manabe Akihiro]] (''従五位下''). Lord of [[Wakasa Province|Wakasa]]. # [[Manabe Akinobu]] (''従五位下''). Lord of Shūzen. # [[Manabe Akikatsu]] (''従四位下''). Lord of Shimōsa. # [[Manabe Akitomo]] (''従五位下''). Lord of [[Awa Province|Awa]]. # [[Manabe Akimichi]] (''正五位''). Last Daimyō; first viscount. # [[Manabe Akinobu]] (''正三位''). Viscount {{Sfn|Peerage Register|1990|p=194}}. == Genealogy == ;Bold indicates the head of the clan, solid lines denote direct descendants, and dotted lines denote adoption. :Note: Some sources attribute the Manabe lineage to the [[Fujiwara clan]], specifically the Northern Fujiwara's Yamakage line. {{familytree/start}} {{familytree |01|藤原山蔭|01|[[Fujiwara no Yamakage|Yamakage]]}} {{familytree | |!||||||}} {{familytree |01|中正|01|[[Fujiwara no Nakazumi|Nakazumi]]}} {{familytree | |!||||||}} {{familytree |01|塩川満任|01|[[Shiokawa Mitsutō|Mitsutō]]}} {{familytree | |!||||||}} {{familytree |01|満任|02|[[Manabe Sadaaki|Sadaaki]]}} {{familytree | |!||||||}} {{familytree |01|詮光|01|[[Manabe Akimitsu|Akimitsu]]}} {{familytree | |!||||||}} {{familytree |01|詮房|02|[[Manabe Akifusa|Akifusa]]}} {{familytree | |:||||||}} {{familytree |01|詮勝|01|[[Manabe Akikatsu|Akikatsu]]}} {{familytree/end}} == References == * Kansei Genealogies (1923), Vol. 8. [[Category:History of Fukui Prefecture]] [[Category:Sabaemabe family]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Manabe clan]] [[Category:Clans in the Kanto region]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Manabe clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Manabe clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Manabe clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Manabe clan]][[de:Manabe (Klan)]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[sn:Manabe clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Manabe clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Manabe clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Manabe clan]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:間部氏]] {{Infobox Japanese clan | name = Manabe Clan | crest = Marunimitsuhikiryo.svg | crest caption = [[Mitsuhiki-ryō within a circle|Maru ni Mitsuhiki]] | home province = [[Echizen Province|Echizen]] ([[Sabae Domain|Nishi Sabae]]) | main branch = [[Manabe clan (main branch)|Main]] | founder = [[Manabe Akimitsu]] | dissolved = | type = [[Samurai]]
[[Kazoku]] (''[[Shishaku]]'') | final ruler = [[Manabe Akinobu]] | noteworthy members = [[Manabe Akifusa]], [[Manabe Akikatsu]] | branches = Honjo Manabe clan ([[Samurai]])
Akasaka Manabe clan ([[Samurai]]) }} The '''Manabe clan''' (間部氏, ''Manabe-shi'') was a [[Samurai]] and later [[Kazoku]] clan of [[Japan]]. Its rise to prominence began in the mid-[[Edo period]] with the elevation of [[Manabe Akifusa]] to [[daimyō]] status. Following the [[Meiji Restoration]], the family became part of the [[Kazoku]] peerage system as ''shishaku'' (''viscount''). The clan's hereditary title included the character 詮 (''sen''). == History == Originally, the clan bore the name "Shiokawa" {{Sfn|Kansei Genealogies|1923|p=708}}. Under Okabe Akimitsu, they adopted "Manabe" after his mother's family, later altering the name to "Manabe" {{Sfn|Kansei Genealogies|1923|p=708}}. During the generation of Kiyosada, a descendant of Akimitsu, the family temporarily adopted the surnames Hoshino and Nishida before reverting to Manabe {{Sfn|Kansei Genealogies|1923|p=708}}. Manabe Akimitsu's father, Manabe Sadaaki, is believed to have been part of a branch of the Manabe family, which ruled the local stronghold in [[Izumi Province]], at Tannowa. Kiyosada's eldest son, Akifusa, served under Tokugawa Tsunayoshi (later [[Tokugawa Ienobu|Ienobu]]), during which time the surname officially changed to Manabe. As Ienobu became shōgun, Akifusa rose as a powerful aide, but his influence declined following the death of the 7th shōgun, [[Tokugawa Ietsugu]]. With the ascension of [[Tokugawa Yoshimune]], Akifusa was reassigned to the [[Murakami Domain]] in [[Echigo Province]]. His successor, [[Manabe Akitoki]], was later transferred to the [[Sabae Domain]] in Echizen Province, marking the clan's final territorial base. Key figures in the clan's history include Akifusa, a prominent shogunal advisor, and [[Manabe Akikatsu]], who served as a senior [[Rōjū|Elder of the Bakufu]]. The last daimyō, [[Manabe Akimichi]], oversaw the clan's transition during the [[Meiji period]]. After the [[Abolition of the han system|abolition of the domains]] in 1871, the Manabe family was incorporated into the Kazoku system. In 1884, under the [[Kazoku|Kazoku peerage system]], the family received the rank of ''shishaku'' (viscount) {{Sfn|Kodate|2006|p=336}}. Under [[Manabe Akinobu]], the family resided in [[Hamadera]], [[Osaka Prefecture]] {{Sfn|Peerage Register|1990|p=194}}. == Lineage of Heads == # [[Manabe Akifusa]] (''従四位下''). Lord of [[Echizen Province|Echizen]]. # [[Manabe Akitoki]] (''従五位下''). Lord of [[Shimōsa Province|Shimōsa]]. # [[Manabe Akimichi]] (''従五位下''). Lord of [[Tango Province|Tango]]. # [[Manabe Akinaka]] (''従五位下''). Lord of [[Shūzen]]. # [[Manabe Akishige]] (''従五位下''). Lord of Shimōsa. # [[Manabe Akihiro]] (''従五位下''). Lord of [[Wakasa Province|Wakasa]]. # [[Manabe Akinobu]] (''従五位下''). Lord of Shūzen. # [[Manabe Akikatsu]] (''従四位下''). Lord of Shimōsa. # [[Manabe Akitomo]] (''従五位下''). Lord of [[Awa Province|Awa]]. # [[Manabe Akimichi]] (''正五位''). Last Daimyō; first viscount. # [[Manabe Akinobu]] (''正三位''). Viscount {{Sfn|Peerage Register|1990|p=194}}. == Genealogy == ;Bold indicates the head of the clan, solid lines denote direct descendants, and dotted lines denote adoption. :Note: Some sources attribute the Manabe lineage to the [[Fujiwara clan]], specifically the Northern Fujiwara's Yamakage line. {{familytree/start}} {{familytree |01|藤原山蔭|01|[[Fujiwara no Yamakage|Yamakage]]}} {{familytree | |!||||||}} {{familytree |01|中正|01|[[Fujiwara no Nakazumi|Nakazumi]]}} {{familytree | |!||||||}} {{familytree |01|塩川満任|01|[[Shiokawa Mitsutō|Mitsutō]]}} {{familytree | |!||||||}} {{familytree |01|満任|02|[[Manabe Sadaaki|Sadaaki]]}} {{familytree | |!||||||}} {{familytree |01|詮光|01|[[Manabe Akimitsu|Akimitsu]]}} {{familytree | |!||||||}} {{familytree |01|詮房|02|[[Manabe Akifusa|Akifusa]]}} {{familytree | |:||||||}} {{familytree |01|詮勝|01|[[Manabe Akikatsu|Akikatsu]]}} {{familytree/end}} == References == * Kansei Genealogies (1923), Vol. 8. Digital archive by the National Diet Library. * Kodate, Yūji. ''Kazoku: The Aristocracy of Modern Japan''. Chūō Kōronsha, 2006. * Peerage Register, 1990 Edition. Nihon Toshokan Center. * Sabae City Historical Archives, ''Manabe Family Documents'', Vol. 1, 1980. == External Links == * {{Kotobank|Manabe clan|2=改訂新版　[[世界大百科事典]]}} [[Category:qq]] [[Category:suQqqq]][[sn:Manabe clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Manabe clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Manabe clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Manabe clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Manabe clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Manabe clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Manabe clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:anabe clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Manabe clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Manabe clan]][[Category:qq]]

Under Okabe Akimitsu, they adopted "Manabe" after his mother's family, later altering the name to "Manabe" {{Sfn|Kansei Genealogies|1923|p=708}}. During the generation of Kiyosada, a descendant of Akimitsu, the family temporarily adopted the surnames Hoshino and Nishida before reverting to Manabe {{Sfn|Kansei Genealogies|1923|p=708}}. Manabe Akimitsu's father, Manabe Sadaaki, is believed to have been part of a branch of the Manabe family, which ruled the local stronghold in [[Izumi Province]], at Tannowa. Kiyosada's eldest son, Akifusa, served under Tokugawa Tsunayoshi (later [[Tokugawa Ienobu|Ienobu]]), during which time the surname officially changed to Manabe. As Ienobu became shōgun, Akifusa rose as a powerful aide, but his influence declined following the death of the 7th shōgun, [[Tokugawa Ietsugu]]. With the ascension of [[Tokugawa Yoshimune]], Akifusa was reassigned to the [[Murakami Domain]] in [[Echigo Province]]. His successor, [[Manabe Akitoki]], was later transferred to the [[Sabae Domain]] in Echizen Province, marking the clan's final territorial base. Key figures in the clan's history include Akifusa, a prominent shogunal advisor, and [[Manabe Akikatsu]], who served as a senior [[Rōjū|Elder of the Bakufu]]. The last daimyō, [[Manabe Akimichi]], oversaw the clan's transition during the [[Meiji period]]. After the [[Abolition of the han system|abolition of the domains]] in 1871, the Manabe family was incorporated into the Kazoku system. In 1884, under the [[Kazoku|Kazoku peerage system]], the family received the rank of ''shishaku'' (viscount) {{Sfn|Kodate|2006|p=336}}. Under [[Manabe Akinobu]], the family resided in [[Hamadera]], [[Osaka Prefecture]] {{Sfn|Peerage Register|1990|p=194}}. == Lineage of Heads == # [[Manabe Akifusa]] (''従四位下''). Lord of [[Echizen Province|Echizen]]. # [[Manabe Akitoki]] (''従五位下''). Lord of [[Shimōsa Province|Shimōsa]]. # [[Manabe Akimichi]] (''従五位下''). Lord of [[Tango Province|Tango]]. # [[Manabe Akinaka]] (''従五位下''). Lord of [[Shūzen]]. # [[Manabe Akishige]] (''従五位下''). Lord of Shimōsa. # [[Manabe Akihiro]] (''従五位下''). Lord of [[Wakasa Province|Wakasa]]. # [[Manabe Akinobu]] (''従五位下''). Lord of Shūzen. # [[Manabe Akikatsu]] (''従四位下''). Lord of Shimōsa. # [[Manabe Akitomo]] (''従五位下''). Lord of [[Awa Province|Awa]]. # [[Manabe Akimichi]] (''正五位''). Last Daimyō; first viscount. # [[Manabe Akinobu]] (''正三位''). Viscount {{Sfn|Peerage Register|1990|p=194}}. == Genealogy == ;Bold indicates the head of the clan, solid lines denote direct descendants, and dotted lines denote adoption. :Note: Some sources attribute the Manabe lineage to the [[Fujiwara clan]], specifically the Northern Fujiwara's Yamakage line. {{familytree/start}} {{familytree |01|藤原山蔭|01|[[Fujiwara no Yamakage|Yamakage]]}} {{familytree | |!||||||}} {{familytree |01|中正|01|[[Fujiwara no Nakazumi|Nakazumi]]}} {{familytree | |!||||||}} {{familytree |01|塩川満任|01|[[Shiokawa Mitsutō|Mitsutō]]}} {{familytree | |!||||||}} {{familytree |01|満任|02|[[Manabe Sadaaki|Sadaaki]]}} {{familytree | |!||||||}} {{familytree |01|詮光|01|[[Manabe Akimitsu|Akimitsu]]}} {{familytree | |!||||||}} {{familytree |01|詮房|02|[[Manabe Akifusa|Akifusa]]}} {{familytree | |:||||||}} {{familytree |01|詮勝|01|[[Manabe Akikatsu|Akikatsu]]}} {{familytree/end}} == References == * Kansei Genealogies (1923), Vol. 8. [[Category:History of Fukui Prefecture]] [[Category:Sabaemabe family]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Manabe clan]] [[Category:Clans in the Kanto region]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Manabe clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Manabe clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Manabe clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Manabe clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Manabe clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Manabe clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Manabe clan]][[de:Manabe (Klan)]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[sn:Manabe clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Manabe clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Manabe clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Manabe clan]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:間部氏]] {{Infobox Japanese clan | name = Manabe Clan | crest = Marunimitsuhikiryo.svg | crest caption = [[Mitsuhiki-ryō within a circle|Maru ni Mitsuhiki]] | home province = [[Echizen Province|Echizen]] ([[Sabae Domain|Nishi Sabae]]) | main branch = [[Manabe clan (main branch)|Main]] | founder = [[Manabe Akimitsu]] | dissolved = | type = [[Samurai]]
[[Kazoku]] (''[[Shishaku]]'') | final ruler = [[Manabe Akinobu]] | noteworthy members = [[Manabe Akifusa]], [[Manabe Akikatsu]] | branches = Honjo Manabe clan ([[Samurai]])
Akasaka Manabe clan ([[Samurai]]) }} The '''Manabe clan''' (間部氏, ''Manabe-shi'') was a [[Samurai]] and later [[Kazoku]] clan of [[Japan]]. Its rise to prominence began in the mid-[[Edo period]] with the elevation of [[Manabe Akifusa]] to [[daimyō]] status. Following the [[Meiji Restoration]], the family became part of the [[Kazoku]] peerage system as ''shishaku'' (''viscount''). The clan's hereditary title included the character 詮 (''sen''). == History == Originally, the clan bore the name "Shiokawa" {{Sfn|Kansei Genealogies|1923|p=708}}. Under Okabe Akimitsu, they adopted "Manabe" after his mother's family, later altering the name to "Manabe" {{Sfn|Kansei Genealogies|1923|p=708}}. During the generation of Kiyosada, a descendant of Akimitsu, the family temporarily adopted the surnames Hoshino and Nishida before reverting to Manabe {{Sfn|Kansei Genealogies|1923|p=708}}. Manabe Akimitsu's father, Manabe Sadaaki, is believed to have been part of a branch of the Manabe family, which ruled the local stronghold in [[Izumi Province]], at Tannowa. Kiyosada's eldest son, Akifusa, served under Tokugawa Tsunayoshi (later [[Tokugawa Ienobu|Ienobu]]), during which time the surname officially changed to Manabe. As Ienobu became shōgun, Akifusa rose as a powerful aide, but his influence declined following the death of the 7th shōgun, [[Tokugawa Ietsugu]]. With the ascension of [[Tokugawa Yoshimune]], Akifusa was reassigned to the [[Murakami Domain]] in [[Echigo Province]]. His successor, [[Manabe Akitoki]], was later transferred to the [[Sabae Domain]] in Echizen Province, marking the clan's final territorial base. Key figures in the clan's history include Akifusa, a prominent shogunal advisor, and [[Manabe Akikatsu]], who served as a senior [[Rōjū|Elder of the Bakufu]]. The last daimyō, [[Manabe Akimichi]], oversaw the clan's transition during the [[Meiji period]]. After the [[Abolition of the han system|abolition of the domains]] in 1871, the Manabe family was incorporated into the Kazoku system. In 1884, under the [[Kazoku|Kazoku peerage system]], the family received the rank of ''shishaku'' (viscount) {{Sfn|Kodate|2006|p=336}}. Under [[Manabe Akinobu]], the family resided in [[Hamadera]], [[Osaka Prefecture]] {{Sfn|Peerage Register|1990|p=194}}. == Lineage of Heads == # [[Manabe Akifusa]] (''従四位下''). Lord of [[Echizen Province|Echizen]]. # [[Manabe Akitoki]] (''従五位下''). Lord of [[Shimōsa Province|Shimōsa]]. # [[Manabe Akimichi]] (''従五位下''). Lord of [[Tango Province|Tango]]. # [[Manabe Akinaka]] (''従五位下''). Lord of [[Shūzen]]. # [[Manabe Akishige]] (''従五位下''). Lord of Shimōsa. # [[Manabe Akihiro]] (''従五位下''). Lord of [[Wakasa Province|Wakasa]]. # [[Manabe Akinobu]] (''従五位下''). Lord of Shūzen. # [[Manabe Akikatsu]] (''従四位下''). Lord of Shimōsa. # [[Manabe Akitomo]] (''従五位下''). Lord of [[Awa Province|Awa]]. # [[Manabe Akimichi]] (''正五位''). Last Daimyō; first viscount. # [[Manabe Akinobu]] (''正三位''). Viscount {{Sfn|Peerage Register|1990|p=194}}. == Genealogy == ;Bold indicates the head of the clan, solid lines denote direct descendants, and dotted lines denote adoption. :Note: Some sources attribute the Manabe lineage to the [[Fujiwara clan]], specifically the Northern Fujiwara's Yamakage line. {{familytree/start}} {{familytree |01|藤原山蔭|01|[[Fujiwara no Yamakage|Yamakage]]}} {{familytree | |!||||||}} {{familytree |01|中正|01|[[Fujiwara no Nakazumi|Nakazumi]]}} {{familytree | |!||||||}} {{familytree |01|塩川満任|01|[[Shiokawa Mitsutō|Mitsutō]]}} {{familytree | |!||||||}} {{familytree |01|満任|02|[[Manabe Sadaaki|Sadaaki]]}} {{familytree | |!||||||}} {{familytree |01|詮光|01|[[Manabe Akimitsu|Akimitsu]]}} {{familytree | |!||||||}} {{familytree |01|詮房|02|[[Manabe Akifusa|Akifusa]]}} {{familytree | |:||||||}} {{familytree |01|詮勝|01|[[Manabe Akikatsu|Akikatsu]]}} {{familytree/end}} == References == * Kansei Genealogies (1923), Vol. 8. Digital archive by the National Diet Library. * Kodate, Yūji. ''Kazoku: The Aristocracy of Modern Japan''. Chūō Kōronsha, 2006. * Peerage Register, 1990 Edition. Nihon Toshokan Center. * Sabae City Historical Archives, ''Manabe Family Documents'', Vol. 1, 1980. == External Links == * {{Kotobank|Manabe clan|2=改訂新版　[[世界大百科事典]]}} [[Category:qq]] [[sn:Manabe clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Manabe clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Manabe clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Manabe clan]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Manabe clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Manabe clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Manabe clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:anabe clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Manabe clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Manabe clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Manabe clan]][[Category:qq]]

revision.text:
[[Category:History of Fukui Prefecture]] [[Category:Sabaemabe family]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Manabe clan]] [[Category:Clans in the Kanto region]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Manabe clan]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Manabe clan]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Manabe clan]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Manabe clan]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Manabe clan]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Manabe clan]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Manabe clan]][[de:Manabe (Klan)]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Manabe clan]][[Category:11]][[sn:Manabe clan]][[Category:11]][[sn:Manabe clan]][[Category:11]][[sn:Manabe clan]][[Category:11]] [[ja:間部氏]] {{Infobox Japanese clan | name = Manabe Clan | crest = Marunimitsuhikiryo.svg | crest caption = [[Mitsuhiki-ryō within a circle|Maru ni Mitsuhiki]] | home province = [[Echizen Province|Echizen]] ([[Sabae Domain|Nishi Sabae]]) | main branch = [[Manabe clan (main branch)|Main]] | founder = [[Manabe Akimitsu]] | dissolved = | type = [[Samurai]]
[[Kazoku]] (''[[Shishaku]]'') | final ruler = [[Manabe Akinobu]] | noteworthy members = [[Manabe Akifusa]], [[Manabe Akikatsu]] | branches = Honjo Manabe clan ([[Samurai]])
Akasaka Manabe clan ([[Samurai]]) }} The '''Manabe clan''' (間部氏, ''Manabe-shi'') was a [[Samurai]] and later [[Kazoku]] clan of [[Japan]]. Its rise to prominence began in the mid-[[Edo period]] with the elevation of [[Manabe Akifusa]] to [[daimyō]] status. Following the [[Meiji Restoration]], the family became part of the [[Kazoku]] peerage system as ''shishaku'' (''viscount''). The clan's hereditary title included the character 詮 (''sen''). == History == Originally, the clan bore the name "Shiokawa" {{Sfn|Kansei Genealogies|1923|p=708}}. Under Okabe Akimitsu, they adopted "Manabe" after his mother's family, later altering the name to "Manabe" {{Sfn|Kansei Genealogies|1923|p=708}}. During the generation of Kiyosada, a descendant of Akimitsu, the family temporarily adopted the surnames Hoshino and Nishida before reverting to Manabe {{Sfn|Kansei Genealogies|1923|p=708}}. Manabe Akimitsu's father, Manabe Sadaaki, is believed to have been part of a branch of the Manabe family, which ruled the local stronghold in [[Izumi Province]], at Tannowa. Kiyosada's eldest son, Akifusa, served under Tokugawa Tsunayoshi (later [[Tokugawa Ienobu|Ienobu]]), during which time the surname officially changed to Manabe. As Ienobu became shōgun, Akifusa rose as a powerful aide, but his influence declined following the death of the 7th shōgun, [[Tokugawa Ietsugu]]. With the ascension of [[Tokugawa Yoshimune]], Akifusa was reassigned to the [[Murakami Domain]] in [[Echigo Province]]. His successor, [[Manabe Akitoki]], was later transferred to the [[Sabae Domain]] in Echizen Province, marking the clan's final territorial base. Key figures in the clan's history include Akifusa, a prominent shogunal advisor, and [[Manabe Akikatsu]], who served as a senior [[Rōjū|Elder of the Bakufu]]. The last daimyō, [[Manabe Akimichi]], oversaw the clan's transition during the [[Meiji period]]. After the [[Abolition of the han system|abolition of the domains]] in 1871, the Manabe family was incorporated into the Kazoku system. In 1884, under the [[Kazoku|Kazoku peerage system]], the family received the rank of ''shishaku'' (viscount) {{Sfn|Kodate|2006|p=336}}. Under [[Manabe Akinobu]], the family resided in [[Hamadera]], [[Osaka Prefecture]] {{Sfn|Peerage Register|1990|p=194}}. == Lineage of Heads == # [[Manabe Akifusa]] (''従四位下''). Lord of [[Echizen Province|Echizen]]. # [[Manabe Akitoki]] (''従五位下''). Lord of [[Shimōsa Province|Shimōsa]]. # [[Manabe Akimichi]] (''従五位下''). Lord of [[Tango Province|Tango]]. # [[Manabe Akinaka]] (''従五位下''). Lord of [[Shūzen]]. # [[Manabe Akishige]] (''従五位下''). Lord of Shimōsa. # [[Manabe Akihiro]] (''従五位下''). Lord of [[Wakasa Province|Wakasa]]. # [[Manabe Akinobu]] (''従五位下''). Lord of Shūzen. # [[Manabe Akikatsu]] (''従四位下''). Lord of Shimōsa. # [[Manabe Akitomo]] (''従五位下''). Lord of [[Awa Province|Awa]]. # [[Manabe Akimichi]] (''正五位''). Last Daimyō; first viscount. # [[Manabe Akinobu]] (''正三位''). Viscount {{Sfn|Peerage Register|1990|p=194}}. == Genealogy == ;Bold indicates the head of the clan, solid lines denote direct descendants, and dotted lines denote adoption. :Note: Some sources attribute the Manabe lineage to the [[Fujiwara clan]], specifically the Northern Fujiwara's Yamakage line. {{familytree/start}} {{familytree |01|藤原山蔭|01|[[Fujiwara no Yamakage|Yamakage]]}} {{familytree | |!||||||}} {{familytree |01|中正|01|[[Fujiwara no Nakazumi|Nakazumi]]}} {{familytree | |!||||||}} {{familytree |01|塩川満任|01|[[Shiokawa Mitsutō|Mitsutō]]}} {{familytree | |!||||||}} {{familytree |01|満任|02|[[Manabe Sadaaki|Sadaaki]]}} {{familytree | |!||||||}} {{familytree |01|詮光|01|[[Manabe Akimitsu|Akimitsu]]}} {{familytree | |!||||||}} {{familytree |01|詮房|02|[[Manabe Akifusa|Akifusa]]}} {{familytree | |:||||||}} {{familytree |01|詮勝|01|[[Manabe Akikatsu|Akikatsu]]}} {{familytree/end}} == References == * Kansei Genealogies (1923), Vol. 8. Digital archive by the National Diet Library. * Kodate, Yūji. ''Kazoku: The Aristocracy of Modern Japan''. Chūō Kōronsha, 2006. * Peerage Register, 1990 Edition. Nihon Toshokan Center. * Sabae City Historical Archives, ''Manabe Family Documents'', Vol. 1, 1980. == External Links == * {{Kotobank|Manabe clan|2=改訂新版　[[世界大百科事典]]}} [[Category:11]] [[sn:Manabe clan]][[Category:11]][[sn:Manabe clan]][[Category:11]][[sn:Manabe clan]][[Category:11]][[sn:Manabe clan]][[Category:11]][[sn:Manabe clan]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Manabe clan]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Manabe clan]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:anabe clan]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Manabe clan]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Manabe clan]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[en:Manabe clan]][[Category:11]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Manabe clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Manabe clan]][[Category:qq]]

Akasaka Manabe clan ([[Samurai]]) }} The '''Manabe clan''' (間部氏, ''Manabe-shi'') was a [[Samurai]] and later [[Kazoku]] clan of [[Japan]]. Its rise to prominence began in the mid-[[Edo period]] with the elevation of [[Manabe Akifusa]] to [[daimyō]] status. Following the [[Meiji Restoration]], the family became part of the [[Kazoku]] peerage system as ''shishaku'' (''viscount''). The clan's hereditary title included the character 詮 (''sen''). == History == Originally, the clan bore the name "Shiokawa" {{Sfn|Kansei Genealogies|1923|p=708}}. Under Okabe Akimitsu, they adopted "Manabe" after his mother's family, later altering the name to "Manabe" {{Sfn|Kansei Genealogies|1923|p=708}}. During the generation of Kiyosada, a descendant of Akimitsu, the family temporarily adopted the surnames Hoshino and Nishida before reverting to Manabe {{Sfn|Kansei Genealogies|1923|p=708}}. Manabe Akimitsu's father, Manabe Sadaaki, is believed to have been part of a branch of the Manabe family, which ruled the local stronghold in [[Izumi Province]], at Tannowa. Kiyosada's eldest son, Akifusa, served under Tokugawa Tsunayoshi (later [[Tokugawa Ienobu|Ienobu]]), during which time the surname officially changed to Manabe. As Ienobu became shōgun, Akifusa rose as a powerful aide, but his influence declined following the death of the 7th shōgun, [[Tokugawa Ietsugu]]. With the ascension of [[Tokugawa Yoshimune]], Akifusa was reassigned to the [[Murakami Domain]] in [[Echigo Province]]. His successor, [[Manabe Akitoki]], was later transferred to the [[Sabae Domain]] in Echizen Province, marking the clan's final territorial base. Key figures in the clan's history include Akifusa, a prominent shogunal advisor, and [[Manabe Akikatsu]], who served as a senior [[Rōjū|Elder of the Bakufu]]. The last daimyō, [[Manabe Akimichi]], oversaw the clan's transition during the [[Meiji period]]. After the [[Abolition of the han system|abolition of the domains]] in 1871, the Manabe family was incorporated into the Kazoku system. In 1884, under the [[Kazoku|Kazoku peerage system]], the family received the rank of ''shishaku'' (viscount) {{Sfn|Kodate|2006|p=336}}. Under [[Manabe Akinobu]], the family resided in [[Hamadera]], [[Osaka Prefecture]] {{Sfn|Peerage Register|1990|p=194}}. == Lineage of Heads == # [[Manabe Akifusa]] (''従四位下''). Lord of [[Echizen Province|Echizen]]. # [[Manabe Akitoki]] (''従五位下''). Lord of [[Shimōsa Province|Shimōsa]]. # [[Manabe Akimichi]] (''従五位下''). Lord of [[Tango Province|Tango]]. # [[Manabe Akinaka]] (''従五位下''). Lord of [[Shūzen]]. # [[Manabe Akishige]] (''従五位下''). Lord of Shimōsa. # [[Manabe Akihiro]] (''従五位下''). Lord of [[Wakasa Province|Wakasa]]. # [[Manabe Akinobu]] (''従五位下''). Lord of Shūzen. # [[Manabe Akikatsu]] (''従四位下''). Lord of Shimōsa. # [[Manabe Akitomo]] (''従五位下''). Lord of [[Awa Province|Awa]]. # [[Manabe Akimichi]] (''正五位''). Last Daimyō; first viscount. # [[Manabe Akinobu]] (''正三位''). Viscount {{Sfn|Peerage Register|1990|p=194}}. == Genealogy == ;Bold indicates the head of the clan, solid lines denote direct descendants, and dotted lines denote adoption. :Note: Some sources attribute the Manabe lineage to the [[Fujiwara clan]], specifically the Northern Fujiwara's Yamakage line. {{familytree/start}} {{familytree |01|藤原山蔭|01|[[Fujiwara no Yamakage|Yamakage]]}} {{familytree | |!||||||}} {{familytree |01|中正|01|[[Fujiwara no Nakazumi|Nakazumi]]}} {{familytree | |!||||||}} {{familytree |01|塩川満任|01|[[Shiokawa Mitsutō|Mitsutō]]}} {{familytree | |!||||||}} {{familytree |01|満任|02|[[Manabe Sadaaki|Sadaaki]]}} {{familytree | |!||||||}} {{familytree |01|詮光|01|[[Manabe Akimitsu|Akimitsu]]}} {{familytree | |!||||||}} {{familytree |01|詮房|02|[[Manabe Akifusa|Akifusa]]}} {{familytree | |:||||||}} {{familytree |01|詮勝|01|[[Manabe Akikatsu|Akikatsu]]}} {{familytree/end}} == References == * Kansei Genealogies (1923), Vol. 8. Lord of [[Echizen Province|Echizen]]. # [[Manabe Akitoki]] (''従五位下''). Lord of [[Shimōsa Province|Shimōsa]]. # [[Manabe Akimichi]] (''従五位下''). Lord of [[Tango Province|Tango]]. # [[Manabe Akinaka]] (''従五位下''). Lord of [[Shūzen]]. # [[Manabe Akishige]] (''従五位下''). Lord of Shimōsa. # [[Manabe Akihiro]] (''従五位下''). Lord of [[Wakasa Province|Wakasa]]. # [[Manabe Akinobu]] (''従五位下''). Lord of Shūzen. # [[Manabe Akikatsu]] (''従四位下''). Lord of Shimōsa. # [[Manabe Akitomo]] (''従五位下''). Lord of [[Awa Province|Awa]]. # [[Manabe Akimichi]] (''正五位''). Last Daimyō; first viscount. # [[Manabe Akinobu]] (''正三位''). Viscount {{Sfn|Peerage Register|1990|p=194}}. == Genealogy == ;Bold indicates the head of the clan, solid lines denote direct descendants, and dotted lines denote adoption. :Note: Some sources attribute the Manabe lineage to the [[Fujiwara clan]], specifically the Northern Fujiwara's Yamakage line. {{familytree/start}} {{familytree |01|藤原山蔭|01|[[Fujiwara no Yamakage|Yamakage]]}} {{familytree | |!||||||}} {{familytree |01|中正|01|[[Fujiwara no Nakazumi|Nakazumi]]}} {{familytree | |!||||||}} {{familytree |01|塩川満任|01|[[Shiokawa Mitsutō|Mitsutō]]}} {{familytree | |!||||||}} {{familytree |01|満任|02|[[Manabe Sadaaki|Sadaaki]]}} {{familytree | |!||||||}} {{familytree |01|詮光|01|[[Manabe Akimitsu|Akimitsu]]}} {{familytree | |!||||||}} {{familytree |01|詮房|02|[[Manabe Akifusa|Akifusa]]}} {{familytree | |:||||||}} {{familytree |01|詮勝|01|[[Manabe Akikatsu|Akikatsu]]}} {{familytree/end}} == References == * Kansei Genealogies (1923), Vol. 8. [[Category:History of Fukui Prefecture]] [[Category:Sabaemabe family]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Manabe clan]] [[Category:Clans in the Kanto region]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category: tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Manabe clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Manabe clan]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Manabe clan]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Manabe clan]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Manabe clan]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Manabe clan]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Manabe clan]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Manabe clan]][[de:Manabe (Klan)]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Manabe clan]][[Category:11]][[sn:Manabe clan]][[Category:11]][[sn:Manabe clan]][[Category:11]][[sn:Manabe clan]][[Category:11]] [[ja:間部氏]] {{Infobox Japanese clan | name = Manabe Clan | crest = Marunimitsuhikiryo.svg | crest caption = [[Mitsuhiki-ryō within a circle|Maru ni Mitsuhiki]] | home province = [[Echizen Province|Echizen]] ([[Sabae Domain|Nishi Sabae]]) | main branch = [[Manabe clan (main branch)|Main]] | founder = [[Manabe Akimitsu]] | dissolved = | type = [[Samurai]]
[[Kazoku]] (''[[Shishaku]]'') | final ruler = [[Manabe Akinobu]] | noteworthy members = [[Manabe Akifusa]], [[Manabe Akikatsu]] | branches = Honjo Manabe clan ([[Samurai]])
Akasaka Manabe clan ([[Samurai]]) }} The '''Manabe clan''' (間部氏, ''Manabe-shi'') was a [[Samurai]] and later [[Kazoku]] clan of [[Japan]]. Its rise to prominence began in the mid-[[Edo period]] with the elevation of [[Manabe Akifusa]] to [[daimyō]] status. Following the [[Meiji Restoration]], the family became part of the [[Kazoku]] peerage system as ''shishaku'' (''viscount''). The clan's hereditary title included the character 詮 (''sen''). == History == Originally, the clan bore the name "Shiokawa" {{Sfn|Kansei Genealogies|1923|p=708}}. Under Okabe Akimitsu, they adopted "Manabe" after his mother's family, later altering the name to "Manabe" {{Sfn|Kansei Genealogies|1923|p=708}}. During the generation of Kiyosada, a descendant of Akimitsu, the family temporarily adopted the surnames Hoshino and Nishida before reverting to Manabe {{Sfn|Kansei Genealogies|1923|p=708}}. Manabe Akimitsu's father, Manabe Sadaaki, is believed to have been part of a branch of the Manabe family, which ruled the local stronghold in [[Izumi Province]], at Tannowa. Kiyosada's eldest son, Akifusa, served under Tokugawa Tsunayoshi (later [[Tokugawa Ienobu|Ienobu]]), during which time the surname officially changed to Manabe. As Ienobu became shōgun, Akifusa rose as a powerful aide, but his influence declined following the death of the 7th shōgun, [[Tokugawa Ietsugu]]. With the ascension of [[Tokugawa Yoshimune]], Akifusa was reassigned to the [[Murakami Domain]] in [[Echigo Province]]. His successor, [[Manabe Akitoki]], was later transferred to the [[Sabae Domain]] in Echizen Province, marking the clan's final territorial base. Key figures in the clan's history include Akifusa, a prominent shogunal advisor, and [[Manabe Akikatsu]], who served as a senior [[Rōjū|Elder of the Bakufu]]. The last daimyō, [[Manabe Akimichi]], oversaw the clan's transition during the [[Meiji period]]. After the [[Abolition of the han system|abolition of the domains]] in 1871, the Manabe family was incorporated into the Kazoku system. In 1884, under the [[Kazoku|Kazoku peerage system]], the family received the rank of ''shishaku'' (viscount) {{Sfn|Kodate|2006|p=336}}. Under [[Manabe Akinobu]], the family resided in [[Hamadera]], [[Osaka Prefecture]] {{Sfn|Peerage Register|1990|p=194}}. == Lineage of Heads == # [[Manabe Akifusa]] (''従四位下''). Lord of [[Echizen Province|Echizen]]. # [[Manabe Akitoki]] (''従五位下''). Lord of [[Shimōsa Province|Shimōsa]]. # [[Manabe Akimichi]] (''従五位下''). Lord of [[Tango Province|Tango]]. # [[Manabe Akinaka]] (''従五位下''). Lord of [[Shūzen]]. # [[Manabe Akishige]] (''従五位下''). Lord of Shimōsa. # [[Manabe Akihiro]] (''従五位下''). Lord of [[Wakasa Province|Wakasa]]. # [[Manabe Akinobu]] (''従五位下''). Lord of Shūzen. # [[Manabe Akikatsu]] (''従四位下''). Lord of Shimōsa. # [[Manabe Akitomo]] (''従五位下''). Lord of [[Awa Province|Awa]]. # [[Manabe Akimichi]] (''正五位''). Last Daimyō; first viscount. # [[Manabe Akinobu]] (''正三位''). Viscount {{Sfn|Peerage Register|1990|p=194}}. == Genealogy == ;Bold indicates the head of the clan, solid lines denote direct descendants, and dotted lines denote adoption. :Note: Some sources attribute the Manabe lineage to the [[Fujiwara clan]], specifically the Northern Fujiwara's Yamakage line. {{familytree/start}} {{familytree |01|藤原山蔭|01|[[Fujiwara no Yamakage|Yamakage]]}} {{familytree | |!||||||}} {{familytree |01|中正|01|[[Fujiwara no Nakazumi|Nakazumi]]}} {{familytree | |!||||||}} {{familytree |01|塩川満任|01|[[Shiokawa Mitsutō|Mitsutō]]}} {{familytree | |!||||||}} {{familytree |01|満任|02|[[Manabe Sadaaki|Sadaaki]]}} {{familytree | |!||||||}} {{familytree |01|詮光|01|[[Manabe Akimitsu|Akimitsu]]}} {{familytree | |!||||||}} {{familytree |01|詮房|02|[[Manabe Akifusa|Akifusa]]}} {{familytree | |:||||||}} {{familytree |01|詮勝|01|[[Manabe Akikatsu|Akikatsu]]}} {{familytree/end}} == References == * Kansei Genealogies (1923), Vol. 8. Digital archive by the National Diet Library. * Kodate, Yūji. ''Kazoku: The Aristocracy of Modern Japan''. Chūō Kōronsha, 2006. * Peerage Register, 1990 Edition. Nihon Toshokan Center. * Sabae City Historical Archives, ''Manabe Family Documents'', Vol. 1, 1980. == External Links == * {{Kotobank|Manabe clan|2=改訂新版　[[世界大百科事典]]}} [[Category:11]] [[sn:Manabe clan]][[Category:11]][[sn:Manabe clan]][[Category:11]][[sn:Manabe clan]][[Category:11]][[sn:Manabe clan]][[Category:11]][[sn:Manabe clan]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Manabe clan]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Manabe clan]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:anabe clan]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Manabe clan]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Manabe clan]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[en:Manabe clan]][[Category:11]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Manabe clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Manabe clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Manabe clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Manabe clan]][[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]][[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]][[Category:qq]]

Manabe Akimitsu's father, Manabe Sadaaki, is believed to have been part of a branch of the Manabe family, which ruled the local stronghold in [[Izumi Province]], at Tannowa. Kiyosada's eldest son, Akifusa, served under Tokugawa Tsunayoshi (later [[Tokugawa Ienobu|Ienobu]]), during which time the surname officially changed to Manabe. As Ienobu became shōgun, Akifusa rose as a powerful aide, but his influence declined following the death of the 7th shōgun, [[Tokugawa Ietsugu]]. With the ascension of [[Tokugawa Yoshimune]], Akifusa was reassigned to the [[Murakami Domain]] in [[Echigo Province]]. His successor, [[Manabe Akitoki]], was later transferred to the [[Sabae Domain]] in Echizen Province, marking the clan's final territorial base. Key figures in the clan's history include Akifusa, a prominent shogunal advisor, and [[Manabe Akikatsu]], who served as a senior [[Rōjū|Elder of the Bakufu]]. The last daimyō, [[Manabe Akimichi]], oversaw the clan's transition during the [[Meiji period]]. After the [[Abolition of the han system|abolition of the domains]] in 1871, the Manabe family was incorporated into the Kazoku system. In 1884, under the [[Kazoku|Kazoku peerage system]], the family received the rank of ''shishaku'' (viscount) {{Sfn|Kodate|2006|p=336}}. Under [[Manabe Akinobu]], the family resided in [[Hamadera]], [[Osaka Prefecture]] {{Sfn|Peerage Register|1990|p=194}}. == Lineage of Heads == # [[Manabe Akifusa]] (''従四位下''). Lord of [[Echizen Province|Echizen]]. # [[Manabe Akitoki]] (''従五位下''). Lord of [[Shimōsa Province|Shimōsa]]. # [[Manabe Akimichi]] (''従五位下''). Lord of [[Tango Province|Tango]]. # [[Manabe Akinaka]] (''従五位下''). Lord of [[Shūzen]]. # [[Manabe Akishige]] (''従五位下''). Lord of Shimōsa. # [[Manabe Akihiro]] (''従五位下''). Lord of [[Wakasa Province|Wakasa]]. # [[Manabe Akinobu]] (''従五位下''). Lord of Shūzen. # [[Manabe Akikatsu]] (''従四位下''). Lord of Shimōsa. # [[Manabe Akitomo]] (''従五位下''). Lord of [[Awa Province|Awa]]. # [[Manabe Akimichi]] (''正五位''). Last Daimyō; first viscount. # [[Manabe Akinobu]] (''正三位''). Viscount {{Sfn|Peerage Register|1990|p=194}}. == Genealogy == ;Bold indicates the head of the clan, solid lines denote direct descendants, and dotted lines denote adoption. :Note: Some sources attribute the Manabe lineage to the [[Fujiwara clan]], specifically the Northern Fujiwara's Yamakage line. {{familytree/start}} {{familytree |01|藤原山蔭|01|[[Fujiwara no Yamakage|Yamakage]]}} {{familytree | |!||||||}} {{familytree |01|中正|01|[[Fujiwara no Nakazumi|Nakazumi]]}} {{familytree | |!||||||}} {{familytree |01|塩川満任|01|[[Shiokawa Mitsutō|Mitsutō]]}} {{familytree | |!||||||}} {{familytree |01|満任|02|[[Manabe Sadaaki|Sadaaki]]}} {{familytree | |!||||||}} {{familytree |01|詮光|01|[[Manabe Akimitsu|Akimitsu]]}} {{familytree | |!||||||}} {{familytree |01|詮房|02|[[Manabe Akifusa|Akifusa]]}} {{familytree | |:||||||}} {{familytree |01|詮勝|01|[[Manabe Akikatsu|Akikatsu]]}} {{familytree/end}} == References == * Kansei Genealogies (1923), Vol. 8. Viscount {{Sfn|Peerage Register|1990|p=194}}. == Genealogy == ;Bold indicates the head of the clan, solid lines denote direct descendants, and dotted lines denote adoption. :Note: Some sources attribute the Manabe lineage to the [[Fujiwara clan]], specifically the Northern Fujiwara's Yamakage line. {{familytree/start}} {{familytree |01|藤原山蔭|01|[[Fujiwara no Yamakage|Yamakage]]}} {{familytree | |!||||||}} {{familytree |01|中正|01|[[Fujiwara no Nakazumi|Nakazumi]]}} {{familytree | |!||||||}} {{familytree |01|塩川満任|01|[[Shiokawa Mitsutō|Mitsutō]]}} {{familytree | |!||||||}} {{familytree |01|満任|02|[[Manabe Sadaaki|Sadaaki]]}} {{familytree | |!||||||}} {{familytree |01|詮光|01|[[Manabe Akimitsu|Akimitsu]]}} {{familytree | |!||||||}} {{familytree |01|詮房|02|[[Manabe Akifusa|Akifusa]]}} {{familytree | |:||||||}} {{familytree |01|詮勝|01|[[Manabe Akikatsu|Akikatsu]]}} {{familytree/end}} == References == * Kansei Genealogies (1923), Vol. 8. revision.text:
{{Infobox Japanese clan | name = Manabe Clan | crest = Marunimitsuhikiryo.svg | crest caption = [[Mitsuhiki-ryō within a circle|Maru ni Mitsuhiki]] | home province = [[Echizen Province|Echizen]] ([[Sabae Domain|Nishi Sabae]]) | main branch = [[Manabe clan (main branch)|Main]] | founder = [[Manabe Akimitsu]] | dissolved = | type = [[Samurai]]
[[Kazoku]] (''[[Shishaku]]'') | final ruler = [[Manabe Akinobu]] | noteworthy members = [[Manabe Akifusa]], [[Manabe Akikatsu]] | branches = Honjo Manabe clan ([[Samurai]])
Akasaka Manabe clan ([[Samurai]]) }} The '''Manabe clan''' (間部氏, ''Manabe-shi'') was a [[Samurai]] and later [[Kazoku]] clan of [[Japan]]. Its rise to prominence began in the mid-[[Edo period]] with the elevation of [[Manabe Akifusa]] to [[daimyō]] status. Following the [[Meiji Restoration]], the family became part of the [[Kazoku]] peerage system as ''shishaku'' (''viscount''). The clan's hereditary title included the character 詮 (''sen''). == History == Originally, the clan bore the name "Shiokawa" {{Sfn|Kansei Genealogies|1923|p=708}}. Under Okabe Akimitsu, they adopted "Manabe" after his mother's family, later altering the name to "Manabe" {{Sfn|Kansei Genealogies|1923|p=708}}. During the generation of Kiyosada, a descendant of Akimitsu, the family temporarily adopted the surnames Hoshino and Nishida before reverting to Manabe {{Sfn|Kansei Genealogies|1923|p=708}}. Manabe Akimitsu's father, Manabe Sadaaki, is believed to have been part of a branch of the Manabe family, which ruled the local stronghold in [[Izumi Province]], at Tannowa. Kiyosada's eldest son, Akifusa, served under Tokugawa Tsunayoshi (later [[Tokugawa Ienobu|Ienobu]]), during which time the surname officially changed to Manabe. As Ienobu became shōgun, Akifusa rose as a powerful aide, but his influence declined following the death of the 7th shōgun, [[Tokugawa Ietsugu]]. With the ascension of [[Tokugawa Yoshimune]], Akifusa was reassigned to the [[Murakami Domain]] in [[Echigo Province]]. His successor, [[Manabe Akitoki]], was later transferred to the [[Sabae Domain]] in Echizen Province, marking the clan's final territorial base. Key figures in the clan's history include Akifusa, a prominent shogunal advisor, and [[Manabe Akikatsu]], who served as a senior [[Rōjū|Elder of the Bakufu]]. The last daimyō, [[Manabe Akimichi]], oversaw the clan's transition during the [[Meiji period]]. After the [[Abolition of the han system|abolition of the domains]] in 1871, the Manabe family was incorporated into the Kazoku system. In 1884, under the [[Kazoku|Kazoku peerage system]], the family received the Rank of ''shishaku'' (viscount) {{Sfn|Kodate|2006|p=336}}. Under [[Manabe Akinobu]], the family resided in [[Hamadera]], [[Osaka Prefecture]] {{Sfn|Peerage Register|1990|p=194}}. == Lineage of Heads == # [[Manabe Akifusa]] (''従四位下''). Lord of [[Echizen Province|Echizen]]. # [[Manabe Akitoki]] (''従五位下''). Lord of [[Shimōsa Province|Shimōsa]]. # [[Manabe Akimichi]] (''従五位下''). Lord of [[Tango Province|Tango]]. # [[Manabe Akinaka]] (''従五位下''). Lord of [[Shūzen]]. # [[Manabe Akishige]] (''従五位下''). Lord of Shimōsa. # [[Manabe Akihiro]] (''従五位下''). Lord of [[Wakasa Province|Wakasa]]. # [[Manabe Akinobu]] (''従五位下''). Lord of Shūzen. # [[Manabe Akikatsu]] (''従四位下''). Lord of Shimōsa. # [[Manabe Akitomo]] (''従五位下''). Lord of [[Awa Province|Awa]]. # [[Manabe Akimichi]] (''正五位''). Last Daimyō; first viscount. # [[Manabe Akinobu]] (''正三位''). Viscount {{Sfn|Peerage Register|1990|p=194}}. == Genealogy == ;Bold indicates the head of the clan, solid lines denote direct descendants, and dotted lines denote adoption. :Note: Some sources attribute the Manabe lineage to the [[Fujiwara clan]], specifically the Northern Fujiwara's Yamakage line. {{familytree/start}} {{familytree |01|藤原山蔭|01|[[Fujiwara no Yamakage|Yamakage]]}} {{familytree | |!||||||}} {{familytree |01|中正|01|[[Fujiwara no Nakazumi|Nakazumi]]}} {{familytree | |!||||||}} {{familytree |01|塩川満任|01|[[Shiokawa Mitsutō|Mitsutō]]}} {{familytree | |!||||||}} {{familytree |01|満任|02|[[Manabe Sadaaki|Sadaaki]]}} {{familytree | |!||||||}} {{familytree |01|詮光|01|[[Manabe Akimitsu|Akimitsu]]}} {{familytree | |!||||||}} {{familytree |01|詮房|02|[[Manabe Akifusa|Akifusa]]}} {{familytree | |:||||||}} {{familytree |01|詮勝|01|[[Manabe Akikatsu|Akikatsu]]}} {{familytree/end}} == References == * Kansei Genealogies (1923), Vol. 8. Digital archive by the National Diet Library. * Kodate, Yūji. ''Kazoku: The Aristocracy of Modern Japan''. Chūō Kōronsha, 2006. * Peerage Register, 1990 Edition. Nihon Toshokan Center. * Sabae City Historical Archives, ''Manabe Family Documents'', Vol. 1, 1980. == External Links == * {{Kotobank|Manabe clan|2=改訂新版　[[世界大百科事典]]}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:History of Fukui Prefecture]] [[Category:Sabaemabe family]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Manabe clan]] [[Category:Clans in the Kanto region]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Manabe clan]] [[de:Manabe (Klan)]] [[ja:間部氏]] [[sn:anabe clan]] [[en:Manabe clan]]

{{Infobox Japanese clan | name = Manabe Clan | crest = Marunimitsuhikiryo.svg | crest caption = [[Mitsuhiki-ryō within a circle|Maru ni Mitsuhiki]] | home province = [[Echizen Province|Echizen]] ([[Sabae Domain|Nishi Sabae]]) | main branch = [[Manabe clan (main branch)|Main]] | founder = [[Manabe Akimitsu]] | dissolved = | type = [[Samurai]]
[[Kazoku]] (''[[Shishaku]]'') | final ruler = [[Manabe Akinobu]] | noteworthy members = [[Manabe Akifusa]], [[Manabe Akikatsu]] | branches = Honjo Manabe clan ([[Samurai]])
Akasaka Manabe clan ([[Samurai]]) }} The '''Manabe clan''' (間部氏, ''Manabe-shi'') was a [[Samurai]] and later [[Kazoku]] clan of [[Japan]]. Its rise to prominence began in the mid-[[Edo period]] with the elevation of [[Manabe Akifusa]] to [[daimyō]] status. Following the [[Meiji Restoration]], the family became part of the [[Kazoku]] peerage system as ''shishaku'' (''viscount''). The clan's hereditary title included the character 詮 (''sen''). == History == Originally, the clan bore the name "Shiokawa" {{Sfn|Kansei Genealogies|1923|p=708}}. Under Okabe Akimitsu, they adopted "Manabe" after his mother's family, later altering the name to "Manabe" {{Sfn|Kansei Genealogies|1923|p=708}}. During the generation of Kiyosada, a descendant of Akimitsu, the family temporarily adopted the surnames Hoshino and Nishida before reverting to Manabe {{Sfn|Kansei Genealogies|1923|p=708}}. Manabe Akimitsu's father, Manabe Sadaaki, is believed to have been part of a branch of the Manabe family, which ruled the local stronghold in [[Izumi Province]], at Tannowa. Kiyosada's eldest son, Akifusa, served under Tokugawa Tsunayoshi (later [[Tokugawa Ienobu|Ienobu]]), during which time the surname officially changed to Manabe. As Ienobu became shōgun, Akifusa rose as a powerful aide, but his influence declined following the death of the 7th shōgun, [[Tokugawa Ietsugu]]. With the ascension of [[Tokugawa Yoshimune]], Akifusa was reassigned to the [[Murakami Domain]] in [[Echigo Province]]. His successor, [[Manabe Akitoki]], was later transferred to the [[Sabae Domain]] in Echizen Province, marking the clan's final territorial base. Key figures in the clan's history include Akifusa, a prominent shogunal advisor, and [[Manabe Akikatsu]], who served as a senior [[Rōjū|Elder of the Bakufu]]. The last daimyō, [[Manabe Akimichi]], oversaw the clan's transition during the [[Meiji period]]. After the [[Abolition of the han system|abolition of the domains]] in 1871, the Manabe family was incorporated into the Kazoku system. In 1884, under the [[Kazoku|Kazoku peerage system]], the family received the Rank of ''shishaku'' (viscount) {{Sfn|Kodate|2006|p=336}}. Under [[Manabe Akinobu]], the family resided in [[Hamadera]], [[Osaka Prefecture]] {{Sfn|Peerage Register|1990|p=194}}. == Lineage of Heads == # [[Manabe Akifusa]] (''従四位下''). Lord of [[Echizen Province|Echizen]]. # [[Manabe Akitoki]] (''従五位下''). Lord of [[Shimōsa Province|Shimōsa]]. # [[Manabe Akimichi]] (''従五位下''). Lord of [[Tango Province|Tango]]. # [[Manabe Akinaka]] (''従五位下''). Lord of [[Shūzen]]. # [[Manabe Akishige]] (''従五位下''). Lord of Shimōsa. # [[Manabe Akihiro]] (''従五位下''). Lord of [[Wakasa Province|Wakasa]]. # [[Manabe Akinobu]] (''従五位下''). Lord of Shūzen. # [[Manabe Akikatsu]] (''従四位下''). Lord of Shimōsa. # [[Manabe Akitomo]] (''従五位下''). Lord of [[Awa Province|Awa]]. # [[Manabe Akimichi]] (''正五位''). Last Daimyō; first viscount. # [[Manabe Akinobu]] (''正三位''). Viscount {{Sfn|Peerage Register|1990|p=194}}. == Genealogy == ;Bold indicates the head of the clan, solid lines denote direct descendants, and dotted lines denote adoption. :Note: Some sources attribute the Manabe lineage to the [[Fujiwara clan]], specifically the Northern Fujiwara's Yamakage line. {{familytree/start}} {{familytree |01|藤原山蔭|01|[[Fujiwara no Yamakage|Yamakage]]}} {{familytree | |!||||||}} {{familytree |01|中正|01|[[Fujiwara no Nakazumi|Nakazumi]]}} {{familytree | |!||||||}} {{familytree |01|塩川満任|01|[[Shiokawa Mitsutō|Mitsutō]]}} {{familytree | |!||||||}} {{familytree |01|満任|02|[[Manabe Sadaaki|Sadaaki]]}} {{familytree | |!||||||}} {{familytree |01|詮光|01|[[Manabe Akimitsu|Akimitsu]]}} {{familytree | |!||||||}} {{familytree |01|詮房|02|[[Manabe Akifusa|Akifusa]]}} {{familytree | |:||||||}} {{familytree |01|詮勝|01|[[Manabe Akikatsu|Akikatsu]]}} {{familytree/end}} == References == * Kansei Genealogies (1923), Vol. 8. Digital archive by the National Diet Library. * Kodate, Yūji. ''Kazoku: The Aristocracy of Modern Japan''. Chūō Kōronsha, 2006. * Peerage Register, 1990 Edition. Nihon Toshokan Center. * Sabae City Historical Archives, ''Manabe Family Documents'', Vol. 1, 1980. == External Links == * {{Kotobank|Manabe clan|2=改訂新版　[[世界大百科事典]]}} {{translated page|ja|間部氏|version=101699610|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:History of Fukui Prefecture]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Clans in the Kanto region]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Manabe clan]] [[de:Manabe (Klan)]] [[ja:間部氏]] [[sn:anabe clan]] [[en:Manabe clan]]

In 1884, under the [[Kazoku|Kazoku peerage system]], the family received the Rank of ''shishaku'' (viscount) {{Sfn|Kodate|2006|p=336}}. Under [[Manabe Akinobu]], the family resided in [[Hamadera]], [[Osaka Prefecture]] {{Sfn|Peerage Register|1990|p=194}}. == Lineage of Heads == # [[Manabe Akifusa]] (''従四位下''). Lord of [[Echizen Province|Echizen]]. # [[Manabe Akitoki]] (''従五位下''). Lord of [[Shimōsa Province|Shimōsa]]. # [[Manabe Akimichi]] (''従五位下''). Lord of [[Tango Province|Tango]]. # [[Manabe Akinaka]] (''従五位下''). Lord of [[Shūzen]]. # [[Manabe Akishige]] (''従五位下''). Lord of Shimōsa. # [[Manabe Akihiro]] (''従五位下''). Lord of [[Wakasa Province|Wakasa]]. # [[Manabe Akinobu]] (''従五位下''). Lord of Shūzen. # [[Manabe Akikatsu]] (''従四位下''). Lord of Shimōsa. # [[Manabe Akitomo]] (''従五位下''). Lord of [[Awa Province|Awa]]. # [[Manabe Akimichi]] (''正五位''). Last Daimyō; first viscount. # [[Manabe Akinobu]] (''正三位''). Viscount {{Sfn|Peerage Register|1990|p=194}}. == Genealogy == ;Bold indicates the head of the clan, solid lines denote direct descendants, and dotted lines denote adoption. :Note: Some sources attribute the Manabe lineage to the [[Fujiwara clan]], specifically the Northern Fujiwara's Yamakage line. {{familytree/start}} {{familytree |01|藤原山蔭|01|[[Fujiwara no Yamakage|Yamakage]]}} {{familytree | |!||||||}} {{familytree |01|中正|01|[[Fujiwara no Nakazumi|Nakazumi]]}} {{familytree | |!||||||}} {{familytree |01|塩川満任|01|[[Shiokawa Mitsutō|Mitsutō]]}} {{familytree | |!||||||}} {{familytree |01|満任|02|[[Manabe Sadaaki|Sadaaki]]}} {{familytree | |!||||||}} {{familytree |01|詮光|01|[[Manabe Akimitsu|Akimitsu]]}} {{familytree | |!||||||}} {{familytree |01|詮房|02|[[Manabe Akifusa|Akifusa]]}} {{familytree | |:||||||}} {{familytree |01|詮勝|01|[[Manabe Akikatsu|Akikatsu]]}} {{familytree/end}} == References == * Kansei Genealogies (1923), Vol. 8. Lord of [[Echizen Province|Echizen]]. # [[Manabe Akitoki]] (''従五位下''). Lord of [[Shimōsa Province|Shimōsa]]. # [[Manabe Akimichi]] (''従五位下''). {{Infobox Japanese clan | name = Manabe Clan | crest = Marunimitsuhikiryo.svg | crest caption = [[Mitsuhiki-ryō within a circle|Maru ni Mitsuhiki]] | home province = [[Echizen Province|Echizen]] ([[Sabae Domain|Nishi Sabae]]) | main branch = [[Manabe clan (main branch)|Main]] | founder = [[Manabe Akimitsu]] | dissolved = | type = [[Samurai]]
[[Kazoku]] (''[[Shishaku]]'') | final ruler = [[Manabe Akinobu]] | noteworthy members = [[Manabe Akifusa]], [[Manabe Akikatsu]] | branches = Honjo Manabe clan ([[Samurai]])
Akasaka Manabe clan ([[Samurai]]) }} The '''Manabe clan''' (間部氏, ''Manabe-shi'') was a [[Samurai]] and later [[Kazoku]] clan of [[Japan]]. Its rise to prominence began in the mid-[[Edo period]] with the elevation of [[Manabe Akifusa]] to [[daimyō]] status. Following the [[Meiji Restoration]], the family became part of the [[Kazoku]] peerage system as ''shishaku'' (''viscount''). The clan's hereditary title included the character 詮 (''sen''). == History == Originally, the clan bore the name "Shiokawa" {{Sfn|Kansei Genealogies|1923|p=708}}. Under Okabe Akimitsu, they adopted "Manabe" after his mother's family, later altering the name to "Manabe" {{Sfn|Kansei Genealogies|1923|p=708}}. During the generation of Kiyosada, a descendant of Akimitsu, the family temporarily adopted the surnames Hoshino and Nishida before reverting to Manabe {{Sfn|Kansei Genealogies|1923|p=708}}. Manabe Akimitsu's father, Manabe Sadaaki, is believed to have been part of a branch of the Manabe family, which ruled the local stronghold in [[Izumi Province]], at Tannowa. Kiyosada's eldest son, Akifusa, served under Tokugawa Tsunayoshi (later [[Tokugawa Ienobu|Ienobu]]), during which time the surname officially changed to Manabe. As Ienobu became shōgun, Akifusa rose as a powerful aide, but his influence declined following the death of the 7th shōgun, [[Tokugawa Ietsugu]]. With the ascension of [[Tokugawa Yoshimune]], Akifusa was reassigned to the [[Murakami Domain]] in [[Echigo Province]]. His successor, [[Manabe Akitoki]], was later transferred to the [[Sabae Domain]] in Echizen Province, marking the clan's final territorial base. Key figures in the clan's history include Akifusa, a prominent shogunal advisor, and [[Manabe Akikatsu]], who served as a senior [[Rōjū|Elder of the Bakufu]]. The last daimyō, [[Manabe Akimichi]], oversaw the clan's transition during the [[Meiji period]]. After the [[Abolition of the han system|abolition of the domains]] in 1871, the Manabe family was incorporated into the Kazoku system. In 1884, under the [[Kazoku|Kazoku peerage system]], the family received the Rank of ''shishaku'' (viscount) {{Sfn|Kodate|2006|p=336}}. Under [[Manabe Akinobu]], the family resided in [[Hamadera]], [[Osaka Prefecture]] {{Sfn|Peerage Register|1990|p=194}}. == Lineage of Heads == # [[Manabe Akifusa]] (''従四位下''). Lord of [[Echizen Province|Echizen]]. # [[Manabe Akitoki]] (''従五位下''). Lord of [[Shimōsa Province|Shimōsa]]. # [[Manabe Akimichi]] (''従五位下''). Lord of [[Tango Province|Tango]]. # [[Manabe Akinaka]] (''従五位下''). Lord of [[Shūzen]]. # [[Manabe Akishige]] (''従五位下''). Lord of Shimōsa. # [[Manabe Akihiro]] (''従五位下''). Lord of [[Wakasa Province|Wakasa]]. # [[Manabe Akinobu]] (''従五位下''). Lord of Shūzen. # [[Manabe Akikatsu]] (''従四位下''). Lord of Shimōsa. # [[Manabe Akitomo]] (''従五位下''). Lord of [[Awa Province|Awa]]. # [[Manabe Akimichi]] (''正五位''). Last Daimyō; first viscount. # [[Manabe Akinobu]] (''正三位''). Viscount {{Sfn|Peerage Register|1990|p=194}}. == Genealogy == ;Bold indicates the head of the clan, solid lines denote direct descendants, and dotted lines denote adoption. :Note: Some sources attribute the Manabe lineage to the [[Fujiwara clan]], specifically the Northern Fujiwara's Yamakage line. {{familytree/start}} {{familytree |01|藤原山蔭|01|[[Fujiwara no Yamakage|Yamakage]]}} {{familytree | |!||||||}} {{familytree |01|中正|01|[[Fujiwara no Nakazumi|Nakazumi]]}} {{familytree | |!||||||}} {{familytree |01|塩川満任|01|[[Shiokawa Mitsutō|Mitsutō]]}} {{familytree | |!||||||}} {{familytree |01|満任|02|[[Manabe Sadaaki|Sadaaki]]}} {{familytree | |!||||||}} {{familytree |01|詮光|01|[[Manabe Akimitsu|Akimitsu]]}} {{familytree | |!||||||}} {{familytree |01|詮房|02|[[Manabe Akifusa|Akifusa]]}} {{familytree | |:||||||}} {{familytree |01|詮勝|01|[[Manabe Akikatsu|Akikatsu]]}} {{familytree/end}} == References == * Kansei Genealogies (1923), Vol. 8. Nihon Toshokan Center. * Sabae City Historical Archives, ''Manabe Family Documents'', Vol. 1, 1980. == External Links == * {{Kotobank|Manabe clan|2=改訂新版　[[世界大百科事典]]}} {{translated page|ja|間部氏|version=101699610|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:History of Fukui Prefecture]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Clans in the Kanto region]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Manabe clan]] [[de:Manabe (Klan)]] [[ja:間部氏]] [[sn:anabe clan]] [[en:Manabe clan]] [[Category:Manabe clan]] [[Category:関東地方の氏族]] [[Category:鯖江間部家]] [[Category:Buke (Adel)]] [[Category:Fujiwara clan]]

{{Infobox Japanese clan | name = Manabe Clan | crest = Marunimitsuhikiryo.svg | crest caption = [[Mitsuhiki-ryō within a circle|Maru ni Mitsuhiki]] | home province = [[Echizen Province|Echizen]] ([[Sabae Domain|Nishi Sabae]]) | main branch = [[Manabe clan (main branch)|Main]] | founder = [[Manabe Akimitsu]] | dissolved = | type = [[Samurai]]
[[Kazoku]] (''[[Shishaku]]'') | final ruler = [[Manabe Akinobu]] | noteworthy members = [[Manabe Akifusa]], [[Manabe Akikatsu]] | branches = Honjo Manabe clan ([[Samurai]])
Akasaka Manabe clan ([[Samurai]]) }} The '''Manabe clan''' (間部氏, ''Manabe-shi'') was a [[Samurai]] and later [[Kazoku]] clan of [[Japan]]. Its rise to prominence began in the mid-[[Edo period]] with the elevation of [[Manabe Akifusa]] to [[daimyō]] status. Following the [[Meiji Restoration]], the family became part of the [[Kazoku]] peerage system as ''shishaku'' (''viscount''). The clan's hereditary title included the character 詮 (''sen''). == History == Originally, the clan bore the name "Shiokawa" {{Sfn|Kansei Genealogies|1923|p=708}}. Under Okabe Akimitsu, they adopted "Manabe" after his mother's family, later altering the name to "Manabe" {{Sfn|Kansei Genealogies|1923|p=708}}. During the generation of Kiyosada, a descendant of Akimitsu, the family temporarily adopted the surnames Hoshino and Nishida before reverting to Manabe {{Sfn|Kansei Genealogies|1923|p=708}}. Manabe Akimitsu's father, Manabe Sadaaki, is believed to have been part of a branch of the Manabe family, which ruled the local stronghold in [[Izumi Province]], at Tannowa. Kiyosada's eldest son, Akifusa, served under Tokugawa Tsunayoshi (later [[Tokugawa Ienobu|Ienobu]]), during which time the surname officially changed to Manabe. As Ienobu became shōgun, Akifusa rose as a powerful aide, but his influence declined following the death of the 7th shōgun, [[Tokugawa Ietsugu]]. With the ascension of [[Tokugawa Yoshimune]], Akifusa was reassigned to the [[Murakami Domain]] in [[Echigo Province]]. His successor, [[Manabe Akitoki]], was later transferred to the [[Sabae Domain]] in Echizen Province, marking the clan's final territorial base. Key figures in the clan's history include Akifusa, a prominent shogunal advisor, and [[Manabe Akikatsu]], who served as a senior [[Rōjū|Elder of the Bakufu]]. The last daimyō, [[Manabe Akimichi]], oversaw the clan's transition during the [[Meiji period]]. After the [[Abolition of the han system|abolition of the domains]] in 1871, the Manabe family was incorporated into the Kazoku system. In 1884, under the [[Kazoku|Kazoku peerage system]], the family received the Rank of ''shishaku'' (viscount) {{Sfn|Kodate|2006|p=336}}. Under [[Manabe Akinobu]], the family resided in [[Hamadera]], [[Osaka Prefecture]] {{Sfn|Peerage Register|1990|p=194}}. == Lineage of Heads == # [[Manabe Akifusa]] (''従四位下''). Lord of [[Echizen Province|Echizen]]. # [[Manabe Akitoki]] (''従五位下''). Lord of [[Shimōsa Province|Shimōsa]]. # [[Manabe Akimichi]] (''従五位下''). Lord of [[Tango Province|Tango]]. # [[Manabe Akinaka]] (''従五位下''). Lord of [[Shūzen]]. # [[Manabe Akishige]] (''従五位下''). Lord of Shimōsa. # [[Manabe Akihiro]] (''従五位下''). Lord of [[Wakasa Province|Wakasa]]. # [[Manabe Akinobu]] (''従五位下''). Lord of Shūzen. # [[Manabe Akikatsu]] (''従四位下''). Lord of Shimōsa. # [[Manabe Akitomo]] (''従五位下''). Lord of [[Awa Province|Awa]]. # [[Manabe Akimichi]] (''正五位''). Last Daimyō; first viscount. # [[Manabe Akinobu]] (''正三位''). Viscount {{Sfn|Peerage Register|1990|p=194}}. == Genealogy == ;Bold indicates the head of the clan, solid lines denote direct descendants, and dotted lines denote adoption. :Note: Some sources attribute the Manabe lineage to the [[Fujiwara clan]], specifically the Northern Fujiwara's Yamakage line. {{familytree/start}} {{familytree |01|藤原山蔭|01|[[Fujiwara no Yamakage|Yamakage]]}} {{familytree | |!||||||}} {{familytree |01|中正|01|[[Fujiwara no Nakazumi|Nakazumi]]}} {{familytree | |!||||||}} {{familytree |01|塩川満任|01|[[Shiokawa Mitsutō|Mitsutō]]}} {{familytree | |!||||||}} {{familytree |01|満任|02|[[Manabe Sadaaki|Sadaaki]]}} {{familytree | |!||||||}} {{familytree |01|詮光|01|[[Manabe Akimitsu|Akimitsu]]}} {{familytree | |!||||||}} {{familytree |01|詮房|02|[[Manabe Akifusa|Akifusa]]}} {{familytree | |:||||||}} {{familytree |01|詮勝|01|[[Manabe Akikatsu|Akikatsu]]}} {{familytree/end}} == References == * Kansei Genealogies (1923), Vol. 8. Digital archive by the National Diet Library. * Kodate, Yūji. ''Kazoku: The Aristocracy of Modern Japan''. Chūō Kōronsha, 2006. * Peerage Register, 1990 Edition. Nihon Toshokan Center. * Sabae City Historical Archives, ''Manabe Family Documents'', Vol. 1, 1980. == External Links == * {{Kotobank|Manabe clan|2=改訂新版　[[世界大百科事典]]}} {{translated page|ja|間部氏|version=101699610|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:History of Fukui Prefecture]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Manabe clan]] [[de:Manabe (Klan)]] [[ja:間部氏]] [[sn:anabe clan]] [[Category:Manabe clan]] [[Category:関東地方の氏族]] [[Category:鯖江間部家]] [[Category:武家の氏族]] [[Category:Fujiwara clan]]
