[[de:Manabe (Klan)]][[ja:間部氏]] [[de:Manabe (Klan)]][[ja:間部氏]] [[de:Manabe (Klan)]][[ja:間部氏]] {{Infobox Japanese clan | name = Manabe Clan | crest = Marunimitsuhikiryo.svg | crest caption = [[Mitsuhiki-ryō within a circle|Maru ni Mitsuhiki]] | home province = {{ill|Echizen Province|en|Echizen Province|WD=Q1047144|lt=Echizen}} ({{ill|Sabae Domain|en|Sabae Domain|WD=Q3034517|lt=Nishi Sabae}}) | main branch = [[Manabe clan (main branch)|Main]] | founder = [[Manabe Akimitsu]] | dissolved = | type = {{ill|Samurai|en|Samurai|WD=Q38142|lt=Samurai}}
[[Kazoku]] (''[[Shishaku]]'') | final ruler = [[Manabe Akinobu]] | noteworthy members = {{ill|Manabe Akifusa|en|Manabe Akifusa|WD=Q6746560|lt=Manabe Akifusa}}, {{ill|Manabe Akikatsu|en|Manabe Akikatsu|WD=Q3285148|lt=Manabe Akikatsu}} | branches = Honjo Manabe clan ({{ill|Samurai|en|Samurai|WD=Q38142|lt=Samurai}})
Akasaka Manabe clan ({{ill|Samurai|en|Samurai|WD=Q38142|lt=Samurai}}) }} The '''Manabe clan''' (間部氏, ''Manabe-shi'') was a {{ill|Samurai|en|Samurai|WD=Q38142|lt=Samurai}} and later [[Kazoku]] clan of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. Its rise to prominence began in the mid-{{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}} with the elevation of {{ill|Manabe Akifusa|en|Manabe Akifusa|WD=Q6746560|lt=Manabe Akifusa}} to [[:en:Daimyō|Daimyō]] status. Following the [[Meiji Restoration]], the family became part of the [[Kazoku]] peerage system as ''shishaku'' (''viscount''). The clan's hereditary title included the character 詮 (''sen''). == History == Originally, the clan bore the name "Shiokawa" {{Sfn|Kansei Genealogies|1923|p=708}}. Under Okabe Akimitsu, they adopted "Manabe" after his mother's family, later altering the name to "Manabe" {{Sfn|Kansei Genealogies|1923|p=708}}. During the generation of Kiyosada, a descendant of Akimitsu, the family temporarily adopted the surnames Hoshino and Nishida before reverting to Manabe {{Sfn|Kansei Genealogies|1923|p=708}}. Manabe Akimitsu's father, Manabe Sadaaki, is believed to have been part of a branch of the Manabe family, which ruled the local stronghold in {{ill|Izumi Province|en|Izumi Province|WD=Q1047117|lt=Izumi Province}}, at Tannowa. Kiyosada's eldest son, Akifusa, served under Tokugawa Tsunayoshi (later {{ill|Tokugawa Ienobu|en|Tokugawa Ienobu|WD=Q366734|lt=Ienobu}}), during which time the surname officially changed to Manabe. As Ienobu became shōgun, Akifusa rose as a powerful aide, but his influence declined following the death of the 7th shōgun, {{ill|Tokugawa Ietsugu|en|Tokugawa Ietsugu|WD=Q366722|lt=Tokugawa Ietsugu}}. With the ascension of {{ill|Tokugawa Yoshimune|en|Tokugawa Yoshimune|WD=Q319664|lt=Tokugawa Yoshimune}}, Akifusa was reassigned to the {{ill|Murakami Domain|en|Murakami Domain|WD=Q849483|lt=Murakami Domain}} in {{ill|Echigo Province|en|Echigo Province|WD=Q666514|lt=Echigo Province}}. His successor, [[:en:Manabe Akitoki|Manabe Akitoki]], was later transferred to the {{ill|Sabae Domain|en|Sabae Domain|WD=Q3034517|lt=Sabae Domain}} in Echizen Province, marking the clan's final territorial base. Key figures in the clan's history include Akifusa, a prominent shogunal advisor, and {{ill|Manabe Akikatsu|en|Manabe Akikatsu|WD=Q3285148|lt=Manabe Akikatsu}}, who served as a senior [[Rōjū|Elder of the Bakufu]]. The last daimyō, [[:en:Manabe Akimichi|Manabe Akimichi]], oversaw the clan's transition during the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}}. After the {{ill|Abolition of the han system|en|Abolition of the han system|WD=Q331905|lt=abolition of the domains}} in 1871, the Manabe family was incorporated into the Kazoku system. In 1884, under the [[Kazoku|Kazoku peerage system]], the family received the Rank of ''shishaku'' (viscount) {{Sfn|Kodate|2006|p=336}}. Under [[Manabe Akinobu]], the family resided in {{ill|Hamadera|en|Hamadera|WD=Q13637275|lt=Hamadera}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture}} {{Sfn|Peerage Register|1990|p=194}}. == Lineage of Heads == # {{ill|Manabe Akifusa|en|Manabe Akifusa|WD=Q6746560|lt=Manabe Akifusa}} (''従四位下''). Lord of {{ill|Echizen Province|en|Echizen Province|WD=Q1047144|lt=Echizen}}. # [[:en:Manabe Akitoki|Manabe Akitoki]] (''従五位下''). Lord of {{ill|Shimōsa Province|en|Shimōsa Province|WD=Q1053695|lt=Shimōsa}}. # [[:en:Manabe Akimichi|Manabe Akimichi]] (''従五位下''). Lord of {{ill|Tango Province|en|Tango Province|WD=Q1054331|lt=Tango}}. # [[:en:Manabe Akinaka|Manabe Akinaka]] (''従五位下''). Lord of [[Shūzen]]. # [[Manabe Akishige]] (''従五位下''). Lord of Shimōsa. # [[Manabe Akihiro]] (''従五位下''). Lord of {{ill|Wakasa Province|en|Wakasa Province|WD=Q1053713|lt=Wakasa}}. # [[Manabe Akinobu]] (''従五位下''). Lord of Shūzen. # {{ill|Manabe Akikatsu|en|Manabe Akikatsu|WD=Q3285148|lt=Manabe Akikatsu}} (''従四位下''). Lord of Shimōsa. # [[Manabe Akitomo]] (''従五位下''). Lord of [[Awa Province|Awa]]. # [[:en:Manabe Akimichi|Manabe Akimichi]] (''正五位''). Last Daimyō; first viscount. # [[Manabe Akinobu]] (''正三位''). Viscount {{Sfn|Peerage Register|1990|p=194}}. == Genealogy == ;Bold indicates the head of the clan, solid lines denote direct descendants, and dotted lines denote adoption. :Note: Some sources attribute the Manabe lineage to the [[Fujiwara clan]], specifically the Northern Fujiwara's Yamakage line. {{familytree/start}} {{familytree |01|藤原山蔭|01|[[Fujiwara no Yamakage|Yamakage]]}} {{familytree | |!||||||}} {{familytree |01|中正|01|[[Fujiwara no Nakazumi|Nakazumi]]}} {{familytree | |!||||||}} {{familytree |01|塩川満任|01|[[Shiokawa Mitsutō|Mitsutō]]}} {{familytree | |!||||||}} {{familytree |01|満任|02|[[Manabe Sadaaki|Sadaaki]]}} {{familytree | |!||||||}} {{familytree |01|詮光|01|[[Manabe Akimitsu|Akimitsu]]}} {{familytree | |!||||||}} {{familytree |01|詮房|02|{{ill|Manabe Akifusa|en|Manabe Akifusa|WD=Q6746560|lt=Akifusa}}}} {{familytree | |:||||||}} {{familytree |01|詮勝|01|{{ill|Manabe Akikatsu|en|Manabe Akikatsu|WD=Q3285148|lt=Akikatsu}}}} {{familytree/end}} == References == * Kansei Genealogies (1923), Vol. 8. Digital archive by the National Diet Library. * Kodate, Yūji. ''Kazoku: The Aristocracy of Modern Japan''. Chūō Kōronsha, 2006. * Peerage Register, 1990 Edition. Nihon Toshokan Center. * Sabae City Historical Archives, ''Manabe Family Documents'', Vol. 1, 1980. == External Links == * {{Kotobank|Manabe clan|2=改訂新版　[[世界大百科事典]]}} {{translated page|ja|間部氏|version=101699610|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[de:Manabe (Klan)]] [[ja:間部氏]] {{wikidata link|Q11655466}} [[Category:Clans of Kantō Region]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Fujiwara clan]] [[Category:History of Fukui Prefecture]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Makabe Family of Sabae]] [[Category:Manabe clan]] [[Category:Samurai Clans of Japan]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

Key figures in the clan's history include Akifusa, a prominent shogunal advisor, and {{ill|Manabe Akikatsu|en|Manabe Akikatsu|WD=Q3285148|lt=Manabe Akikatsu}}, who served as a senior [[Rōjū|Elder of the Bakufu]]. The last daimyō, [[:en:Manabe Akimichi|Manabe Akimichi]], oversaw the clan's transition during the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}}. After the {{ill|Abolition of the han system|en|Abolition of the han system|WD=Q331905|lt=abolition of the domains}} in 1871, the Manabe family was incorporated into the Kazoku system. In 1884, under the [[Kazoku|Kazoku peerage system]], the family received the Rank of ''shishaku'' (viscount) {{Sfn|Kodate|2006|p=336}}. Under [[Manabe Akinobu]], the family resided in {{ill|Hamadera|en|Hamadera|WD=Q13637275|lt=Hamadera}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture}} {{Sfn|Peerage Register|1990|p=194}}. == Lineage of Heads == # {{ill|Manabe Akifusa|en|Manabe Akifusa|WD=Q6746560|lt=Manabe Akifusa}} (''従四位下''). Lord of {{ill|Echizen Province|en|Echizen Province|WD=Q1047144|lt=Echizen}}. # [[:en:Manabe Akitoki|Manabe Akitoki]] (''従五位下''). Lord of {{ill|Shimōsa Province|en|Shimōsa Province|WD=Q1053695|lt=Shimōsa}}. # [[:en:Manabe Akimichi|Manabe Akimichi]] (''従五位下''). Lord of {{ill|Tango Province|en|Tango Province|WD=Q1054331|lt=Tango}}. # [[:en:Manabe Akinaka|Manabe Akinaka]] (''従五位下''). Lord of [[Shūzen]]. # [[Manabe Akishige]] (''従五位下''). Lord of Shimōsa. # [[Manabe Akihiro]] (''従五位下''). Lord of {{ill|Wakasa Province|en|Wakasa Province|WD=Q1053713|lt=Wakasa}}. # [[Manabe Akinobu]] (''従五位下''). Lord of Shūzen. # {{ill|Manabe Akikatsu|en|Manabe Akikatsu|WD=Q3285148|lt=Manabe Akikatsu}} (''従四位下''). Lord of Shimōsa. # [[Manabe Akitomo]] (''従五位下''). Lord of [[Awa Province|Awa]]. # [[:en:Manabe Akimichi|Manabe Akimichi]] (''正五位''). Last Daimyō; first viscount. # [[Manabe Akinobu]] (''正三位''). Viscount {{Sfn|Peerage Register|1990|p=194}}. == Genealogy == ;Bold indicates the head of the clan, solid lines denote direct descendants, and dotted lines denote adoption. :Note: Some sources attribute the Manabe lineage to the [[Fujiwara clan]], specifically the Northern Fujiwara's Yamakage line. {{familytree/start}} {{familytree |01|藤原山蔭|01|[[Fujiwara no Yamakage|Yamakage]]}} {{familytree | |!||||||}} {{familytree |01|中正|01|[[Fujiwara no Nakazumi|Nakazumi]]}} {{familytree | |!||||||}} {{familytree |01|塩川満任|01|[[Shiokawa Mitsutō|Mitsutō]]}} {{familytree | |!||||||}} {{familytree |01|満任|02|[[Manabe Sadaaki|Sadaaki]]}} {{familytree | |!||||||}} {{familytree |01|詮光|01|[[Manabe Akimitsu|Akimitsu]]}} {{familytree | |!||||||}} {{familytree |01|詮房|02|{{ill|Manabe Akifusa|en|Manabe Akifusa|WD=Q6746560|lt=Akifusa}}}} {{familytree | |:||||||}} {{familytree |01|詮勝|01|{{ill|Manabe Akikatsu|en|Manabe Akikatsu|WD=Q3285148|lt=Akikatsu}}}} {{familytree/end}} == References == * Kansei Genealogies (1923), Vol. 8. [[de:Manabe (Klan)]][[ja:間部氏]] [[de:Manabe (Klan)]][[ja:間部氏]] [[de:Manabe (Klan)]][[ja:間部氏]] {{Infobox Japanese clan | name = Manabe Clan | crest = Marunimitsuhikiryo.svg | crest caption = [[Mitsuhiki-ryō within a circle|Maru ni Mitsuhiki]] | home province = {{ill|Echizen Province|en|Echizen Province|WD=Q1047144|lt=Echizen|1=Echizen Province}} ({{ill|Sabae Domain|en|Sabae Domain|WD=Q3034517|lt=Nishi Sabae|1=Sabae Domain}}) | main branch = [[Manabe clan (main branch)|Main]] | founder = [[Manabe Akimitsu]] | dissolved = | type = {{ill|Samurai|en|Samurai|WD=Q38142|lt=Samurai|1=Samurai}}
[[Kazoku]] (''[[Shishaku]]'') | final ruler = [[Manabe Akinobu]] | noteworthy members = {{ill|Manabe Akifusa|en|Manabe Akifusa|WD=Q6746560|lt=Manabe Akifusa|1=Manabe Akifusa}}, {{ill|Manabe Akikatsu|en|Manabe Akikatsu|WD=Q3285148|lt=Manabe Akikatsu|1=Manabe Akikatsu}} | branches = Honjo Manabe clan ({{ill|Samurai|en|Samurai|WD=Q38142|lt=Samurai|1=Samurai}})
Akasaka Manabe clan ({{ill|Samurai|en|Samurai|WD=Q38142|lt=Samurai|1=Samurai}}) }} The '''Manabe clan''' (間部氏, ''Manabe-shi'') was a {{ill|Samurai|en|Samurai|WD=Q38142|lt=Samurai|1=Samurai}} and later [[Kazoku]] clan of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. Its rise to prominence began in the mid-{{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}} with the elevation of {{ill|Manabe Akifusa|en|Manabe Akifusa|WD=Q6746560|lt=Manabe Akifusa|1=Manabe Akifusa}} to [[:en:Daimyō|Daimyō]] status. Following the [[Meiji Restoration]], the family became part of the [[Kazoku]] peerage system as ''shishaku'' (''viscount''). The clan's hereditary title included the character 詮 (''sen''). == History == Originally, the clan bore the name "Shiokawa" {{Sfn|Kansei Genealogies|1923|p=708}}. Under Okabe Akimitsu, they adopted "Manabe" after his mother's family, later altering the name to "Manabe" {{Sfn|Kansei Genealogies|1923|p=708}}. During the generation of Kiyosada, a descendant of Akimitsu, the family temporarily adopted the surnames Hoshino and Nishida before reverting to Manabe {{Sfn|Kansei Genealogies|1923|p=708}}. Manabe Akimitsu's father, Manabe Sadaaki, is believed to have been part of a branch of the Manabe family, which ruled the local stronghold in {{ill|Izumi Province|en|Izumi Province|WD=Q1047117|lt=Izumi Province|1=Izumi Province}}, at Tannowa. Kiyosada's eldest son, Akifusa, served under Tokugawa Tsunayoshi (later {{ill|Tokugawa Ienobu|en|Tokugawa Ienobu|WD=Q366734|lt=Ienobu|1=Tokugawa Ienobu}}), during which time the surname officially changed to Manabe. As Ienobu became shōgun, Akifusa rose as a powerful aide, but his influence declined following the death of the 7th shōgun, {{ill|Tokugawa Ietsugu|en|Tokugawa Ietsugu|WD=Q366722|lt=Tokugawa Ietsugu|1=Tokugawa Ietsugu}}. With the ascension of {{ill|Tokugawa Yoshimune|en|Tokugawa Yoshimune|WD=Q319664|lt=Tokugawa Yoshimune|1=Tokugawa Yoshimune}}, Akifusa was reassigned to the {{ill|Murakami Domain|en|Murakami Domain|WD=Q849483|lt=Murakami Domain|1=Murakami Domain}} in {{ill|Echigo Province|en|Echigo Province|WD=Q666514|lt=Echigo Province|1=Echigo Province}}. His successor, [[:en:Manabe Akitoki|Manabe Akitoki]], was later transferred to the {{ill|Sabae Domain|en|Sabae Domain|WD=Q3034517|lt=Sabae Domain|1=Sabae Domain}} in Echizen Province, marking the clan's final territorial base. Key figures in the clan's history include Akifusa, a prominent shogunal advisor, and {{ill|Manabe Akikatsu|en|Manabe Akikatsu|WD=Q3285148|lt=Manabe Akikatsu|1=Manabe Akikatsu}}, who served as a senior [[Rōjū|Elder of the Bakufu]]. The last daimyō, [[:en:Manabe Akimichi|Manabe Akimichi]], oversaw the clan's transition during the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}}. After the {{ill|Abolition of the han system|en|Abolition of the han system|WD=Q331905|lt=abolition of the domains|1=Abolition of the han system}} in 1871, the Manabe family was incorporated into the Kazoku system. In 1884, under the [[Kazoku|Kazoku peerage system]], the family received the Rank of ''shishaku'' (viscount) {{Sfn|Kodate|2006|p=336}}. Under [[Manabe Akinobu]], the family resided in {{ill|Hamadera|en|Hamadera|WD=Q13637275|lt=Hamadera|1=Hamadera}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture|1=Osaka Prefecture}} {{Sfn|Peerage Register|1990|p=194}}. == Lineage of Heads == # {{ill|Manabe Akifusa|en|Manabe Akifusa|WD=Q6746560|lt=Manabe Akifusa|1=Manabe Akifusa}} (''従四位下''). Lord of {{ill|Echizen Province|en|Echizen Province|WD=Q1047144|lt=Echizen|1=Echizen Province}}. # [[:en:Manabe Akitoki|Manabe Akitoki]] (''従五位下''). Lord of {{ill|Shimōsa Province|en|Shimōsa Province|WD=Q1053695|lt=Shimōsa|1=Shimōsa Province}}. # [[:en:Manabe Akimichi|Manabe Akimichi]] (''従五位下''). Lord of {{ill|Tango Province|en|Tango Province|WD=Q1054331|lt=Tango|1=Tango Province}}. # [[:en:Manabe Akinaka|Manabe Akinaka]] (''従五位下''). Lord of [[Shūzen]]. # [[Manabe Akishige]] (''従五位下''). Lord of Shimōsa. # [[Manabe Akihiro]] (''従五位下''). Lord of {{ill|Wakasa Province|en|Wakasa Province|WD=Q1053713|lt=Wakasa|1=Wakasa Province}}. # [[Manabe Akinobu]] (''従五位下''). Lord of Shūzen. # {{ill|Manabe Akikatsu|en|Manabe Akikatsu|WD=Q3285148|lt=Manabe Akikatsu|1=Manabe Akikatsu}} (''従四位下''). Lord of Shimōsa. # [[Manabe Akitomo]] (''従五位下''). Lord of [[Awa Province|Awa]]. # [[:en:Manabe Akimichi|Manabe Akimichi]] (''正五位''). Last Daimyō; first viscount. # [[Manabe Akinobu]] (''正三位''). Viscount {{Sfn|Peerage Register|1990|p=194}}. == Genealogy == ;Bold indicates the head of the clan, solid lines denote direct descendants, and dotted lines denote adoption. :Note: Some sources attribute the Manabe lineage to the [[Fujiwara clan]], specifically the Northern Fujiwara's Yamakage line. {{familytree/start}} {{familytree |01|藤原山蔭|01|[[Fujiwara no Yamakage|Yamakage]]}} {{familytree | |!||||||}} {{familytree |01|中正|01|[[Fujiwara no Nakazumi|Nakazumi]]}} {{familytree | |!||||||}} {{familytree |01|塩川満任|01|[[Shiokawa Mitsutō|Mitsutō]]}} {{familytree | |!||||||}} {{familytree |01|満任|02|[[Manabe Sadaaki|Sadaaki]]}} {{familytree | |!||||||}} {{familytree |01|詮光|01|[[Manabe Akimitsu|Akimitsu]]}} {{familytree | |!||||||}} {{familytree |01|詮房|02|{{ill|Manabe Akifusa|en|Manabe Akifusa|WD=Q6746560|lt=Akifusa|1=Manabe Akifusa}}}} {{familytree | |:||||||}} {{familytree |01|詮勝|01|{{ill|Manabe Akikatsu|en|Manabe Akikatsu|WD=Q3285148|lt=Akikatsu|1=Manabe Akikatsu}}}} {{familytree/end}} == References == * Kansei Genealogies (1923), Vol. 8. During the generation of Kiyosada, a descendant of Akimitsu, the family temporarily adopted the surnames Hoshino and Nishida before reverting to Manabe {{Sfn|Kansei Genealogies|1923|p=708}}. Kiyosada's eldest son, Akifusa, served under Tokugawa Tsunayoshi (later {{ill|Tokugawa Ienobu|en|Tokugawa Ienobu|WD=Q366734|lt=Ienobu|1=Tokugawa Ienobu}}), during which time the surname officially changed to Manabe. As Ienobu became shōgun, Akifusa rose as a powerful aide, but his influence declined following the death of the 7th shōgun, {{ill|Tokugawa Ietsugu|en|Tokugawa Ietsugu|WD=Q366722|lt=Tokugawa Ietsugu|1=Tokugawa Ietsugu}}. With the ascension of {{ill|Tokugawa Yoshimune|en|Tokugawa Yoshimune|WD=Q319664|lt=Tokugawa Yoshimune|1=Tokugawa Yoshimune}}, Akifusa was reassigned to the {{ill|Murakami Domain|en|Murakami Domain|WD=Q849483|lt=Murakami Domain|1=Murakami Domain}} in {{ill|Echigo Province|en|Echigo Province|WD=Q666514|lt=Echigo Province|1=Echigo Province}}. His successor, [[:en:Manabe Akitoki|Manabe Akitoki]], was later transferred to the {{ill|Sabae Domain|en|Sabae Domain|WD=Q3034517|lt=Sabae Domain|1=Sabae Domain}} in Echizen Province, marking the clan's final territorial base. Key figures in the clan's history include Akifusa, a prominent shogunal advisor, and {{ill|Manabe Akikatsu|en|Manabe Akikatsu|WD=Q3285148|lt=Manabe Akikatsu|1=Manabe Akikatsu}}, who served as a senior [[Rōjū|Elder of the Bakufu]]. The last daimyō, [[:en:Manabe Akimichi|Manabe Akimichi]], oversaw the clan's transition during the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}}. After the {{ill|Abolition of the han system|en|Abolition of the han system|WD=Q331905|lt=abolition of the domains|1=Abolition of the han system}} in 1871, the Manabe family was incorporated into the Kazoku system. In 1884, under the [[Kazoku|Kazoku peerage system]], the family received the Rank of ''shishaku'' (viscount) {{Sfn|Kodate|2006|p=336}}. Under [[Manabe Akinobu]], the family resided in {{ill|Hamadera|en|Hamadera|WD=Q13637275|lt=Hamadera|1=Hamadera}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture|1=Osaka Prefecture}} {{Sfn|Peerage Register|1990|p=194}}. == Lineage of Heads == # {{ill|Manabe Akifusa|en|Manabe Akifusa|WD=Q6746560|lt=Manabe Akifusa|1=Manabe Akifusa}} (''従四位下''). Lord of {{ill|Echizen Province|en|Echizen Province|WD=Q1047144|lt=Echizen|1=Echizen Province}}. # [[:en:Manabe Akitoki|Manabe Akitoki]] (''従五位下''). Lord of {{ill|Shimōsa Province|en|Shimōsa Province|WD=Q1053695|lt=Shimōsa|1=Shimōsa Province}}. # [[:en:Manabe Akimichi|Manabe Akimichi]] (''従五位下''). Lord of {{ill|Tango Province|en|Tango Province|WD=Q1054331|lt=Tango|1=Tango Province}}. # [[:en:Manabe Akinaka|Manabe Akinaka]] (''従五位下''). Lord of [[Shūzen]]. # [[Manabe Akishige]] (''従五位下''). Lord of Shimōsa. # [[Manabe Akihiro]] (''従五位下''). Lord of {{ill|Wakasa Province|en|Wakasa Province|WD=Q1053713|lt=Wakasa|1=Wakasa Province}}. # [[Manabe Akinobu]] (''従五位下''). Lord of Shūzen. # {{ill|Manabe Akikatsu|en|Manabe Akikatsu|WD=Q3285148|lt=Manabe Akikatsu|1=Manabe Akikatsu}} (''従四位下''). Lord of Shimōsa. # [[Manabe Akitomo]] (''従五位下''). Lord of [[Awa Province|Awa]]. # [[:en:Manabe Akimichi|Manabe Akimichi]] (''正五位''). Last Daimyō; first viscount. # [[Manabe Akinobu]] (''正三位''). Viscount {{Sfn|Peerage Register|1990|p=194}}. == Genealogy == ;Bold indicates the head of the clan, solid lines denote direct descendants, and dotted lines denote adoption. :Note: Some sources attribute the Manabe lineage to the [[Fujiwara clan]], specifically the Northern Fujiwara's Yamakage line. {{familytree/start}} {{familytree |01|藤原山蔭|01|[[Fujiwara no Yamakage|Yamakage]]}} {{familytree | |!||||||}} {{familytree |01|中正|01|[[Fujiwara no Nakazumi|Nakazumi]]}} {{familytree | |!||||||}} {{familytree |01|塩川満任|01|[[Shiokawa Mitsutō|Mitsutō]]}} {{familytree | |!||||||}} {{familytree |01|満任|02|[[Manabe Sadaaki|Sadaaki]]}} {{familytree | |!||||||}} {{familytree |01|詮光|01|[[Manabe Akimitsu|Akimitsu]]}} {{familytree | |!||||||}} {{familytree |01|詮房|02|{{ill|Manabe Akifusa|en|Manabe Akifusa|WD=Q6746560|lt=Akifusa|1=Manabe Akifusa}}}} {{familytree | |:||||||}} {{familytree |01|詮勝|01|{{ill|Manabe Akikatsu|en|Manabe Akikatsu|WD=Q3285148|lt=Akikatsu|1=Manabe Akikatsu}}}} {{familytree/end}} == References == * Kansei Genealogies (1923), Vol. 8. Digital archive by the National Diet Library. * Kodate, Yūji. ''Kazoku: The Aristocracy of Modern Japan''. Chūō Kōronsha, 2006. * Peerage Register, 1990 Edition. Nihon Toshokan Center. * Sabae City Historical Archives, ''Manabe Family Documents'', Vol. 1, 1980. == External Links == * {{Kotobank|Manabe clan|2=改訂新版　[[世界大百科事典]]}} {{translated page|ja|間部氏|version=101699610|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[de:Manabe (Klan)]] [[ja:間部氏]] {{wikidata link|Q11655466}} [[Category:Clans of Kantō Region]] [[Category:Fujiwara clan]] [[Category:History of Fukui Prefecture]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Makabe Family of Sabae]] [[Category:Manabe clan]] [[Category:Samurai Clans of Japan]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

[[Kazoku]] (''[[Shishaku]]'') | final ruler = [[Manabe Akinobu]] | noteworthy members = {{ill|Manabe Akifusa|en|Manabe Akifusa|qid=Q6746560|lt=Manabe Akifusa|1=Manabe Akifusa}}, {{ill|Manabe Akikatsu|en|Manabe Akikatsu|qid=Q3285148|lt=Manabe Akikatsu|1=Manabe Akikatsu}} | branches = Honjo Manabe clan ({{ill|Samurai|en|Samurai|qid=Q38142|lt=Samurai|1=Samurai}})
Akasaka Manabe clan ({{ill|Samurai|en|Samurai|qid=Q38142|lt=Samurai|1=Samurai}}) }} The '''Manabe clan''' (間部氏, ''Manabe-shi'') was a {{ill|Samurai|en|Samurai|qid=Q38142|lt=Samurai|1=Samurai}} and later [[Kazoku]] clan of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. Its rise to prominence began in the mid-{{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|qid=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}} with the elevation of {{ill|Manabe Akifusa|en|Manabe Akifusa|qid=Q6746560|lt=Manabe Akifusa|1=Manabe Akifusa}} to [[:en:Daimyō|Daimyō]] status. Following the [[Meiji Restoration]], the family became part of the [[Kazoku]] peerage system as ''shishaku'' (''viscount''). The clan's hereditary title included the character 詮 (''sen''). == History == Originally, the clan bore the name "Shiokawa" {{Sfn|Kansei Genealogies|1923|p=708}}. Under Okabe Akimitsu, they adopted "Manabe" after his mother's family, later altering the name to "Manabe" {{Sfn|Kansei Genealogies|1923|p=708}}. During the generation of Kiyosada, a descendant of Akimitsu, the family temporarily adopted the surnames Hoshino and Nishida before reverting to Manabe {{Sfn|Kansei Genealogies|1923|p=708}}. Manabe Akimitsu's father, Manabe Sadaaki, is believed to have been part of a branch of the Manabe family, which ruled the local stronghold in {{ill|Izumi Province|en|Izumi Province|qid=Q1047117|lt=Izumi Province|1=Izumi Province}}, at Tannowa. Kiyosada's eldest son, Akifusa, served under Tokugawa Tsunayoshi (later {{ill|Tokugawa Ienobu|en|Tokugawa Ienobu|qid=Q366734|lt=Ienobu|1=Tokugawa Ienobu}}), during which time the surname officially changed to Manabe. As Ienobu became shōgun, Akifusa rose as a powerful aide, but his influence declined following the death of the 7th shōgun, {{ill|Tokugawa Ietsugu|en|Tokugawa Ietsugu|qid=Q366722|lt=Tokugawa Ietsugu|1=Tokugawa Ietsugu}}. With the ascension of {{ill|Tokugawa Yoshimune|en|Tokugawa Yoshimune|qid=Q319664|lt=Tokugawa Yoshimune|1=Tokugawa Yoshimune}}, Akifusa was reassigned to the {{ill|Murakami Domain|en|Murakami Domain|qid=Q849483|lt=Murakami Domain|1=Murakami Domain}} in {{ill|Echigo Province|en|Echigo Province|qid=Q666514|lt=Echigo Province|1=Echigo Province}}. His successor, [[:en:Manabe Akitoki|Manabe Akitoki]], was later transferred to the {{ill|Sabae Domain|en|Sabae Domain|qid=Q3034517|lt=Sabae Domain|1=Sabae Domain}} in Echizen Province, marking the clan's final territorial base. Key figures in the clan's history include Akifusa, a prominent shogunal advisor, and {{ill|Manabe Akikatsu|en|Manabe Akikatsu|qid=Q3285148|lt=Manabe Akikatsu|1=Manabe Akikatsu}}, who served as a senior [[Rōjū|Elder of the Bakufu]]. The last daimyō, [[:en:Manabe Akimichi|Manabe Akimichi]], oversaw the clan's transition during the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|qid=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}}. After the {{ill|Abolition of the han system|en|Abolition of the han system|qid=Q331905|lt=abolition of the domains|1=Abolition of the han system}} in 1871, the Manabe family was incorporated into the Kazoku system. In 1884, under the [[Kazoku|Kazoku peerage system]], the family received the Rank of ''shishaku'' (viscount) {{Sfn|Kodate|2006|p=336}}. Under [[Manabe Akinobu]], the family resided in {{ill|Hamadera|en|Hamadera|qid=Q13637275|lt=Hamadera|1=Hamadera}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|qid=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture|1=Osaka Prefecture}} {{Sfn|Peerage Register|1990|p=194}}. == Lineage of Heads == # {{ill|Manabe Akifusa|en|Manabe Akifusa|qid=Q6746560|lt=Manabe Akifusa|1=Manabe Akifusa}} (''従四位下''). Lord of {{ill|Echizen Province|en|Echizen Province|qid=Q1047144|lt=Echizen|1=Echizen Province}}. # [[:en:Manabe Akitoki|Manabe Akitoki]] (''従五位下''). Lord of {{ill|Shimōsa Province|en|Shimōsa Province|qid=Q1053695|lt=Shimōsa|1=Shimōsa Province}}. # [[:en:Manabe Akimichi|Manabe Akimichi]] (''従五位下''). Lord of {{ill|Tango Province|en|Tango Province|qid=Q1054331|lt=Tango|1=Tango Province}}. # [[:en:Manabe Akinaka|Manabe Akinaka]] (''従五位下''). Lord of [[Shūzen]]. # [[Manabe Akishige]] (''従五位下''). Lord of Shimōsa. # [[Manabe Akihiro]] (''従五位下''). Lord of {{ill|Wakasa Province|en|Wakasa Province|qid=Q1053713|lt=Wakasa|1=Wakasa Province}}. # [[Manabe Akinobu]] (''従五位下''). Lord of Shūzen. # {{ill|Manabe Akikatsu|en|Manabe Akikatsu|qid=Q3285148|lt=Manabe Akikatsu|1=Manabe Akikatsu}} (''従四位下''). Lord of Shimōsa. # [[Manabe Akitomo]] (''従五位下''). Lord of [[Awa Province|Awa]]. # [[:en:Manabe Akimichi|Manabe Akimichi]] (''正五位''). Last Daimyō; first viscount. # [[Manabe Akinobu]] (''正三位''). Viscount {{Sfn|Peerage Register|1990|p=194}}. == Genealogy == ;Bold indicates the head of the clan, solid lines denote direct descendants, and dotted lines denote adoption. :Note: Some sources attribute the Manabe lineage to the [[Fujiwara clan]], specifically the Northern Fujiwara's Yamakage line. {{familytree/start}} {{familytree |01|藤原山蔭|01|[[Fujiwara no Yamakage|Yamakage]]}} {{familytree | |!||||||}} {{familytree |01|中正|01|[[Fujiwara no Nakazumi|Nakazumi]]}} {{familytree | |!||||||}} {{familytree |01|塩川満任|01|[[Shiokawa Mitsutō|Mitsutō]]}} {{familytree | |!||||||}} {{familytree |01|満任|02|[[Manabe Sadaaki|Sadaaki]]}} {{familytree | |!||||||}} {{familytree |01|詮光|01|[[Manabe Akimitsu|Akimitsu]]}} {{familytree | |!||||||}} {{familytree |01|詮房|02|{{ill|Manabe Akifusa|en|Manabe Akifusa|qid=Q6746560|lt=Akifusa|1=Manabe Akifusa}}}} {{familytree | |:||||||}} {{familytree |01|詮勝|01|{{ill|Manabe Akikatsu|en|Manabe Akikatsu|qid=Q3285148|lt=Akikatsu|1=Manabe Akikatsu}}}} {{familytree/end}} == References == * Kansei Genealogies (1923), Vol. 8.
